items.value: <u>Subject: Master Circular for SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements Regulations) 2018</u>
items.md: <u>**Subject: Master Circular for SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements Regulations) 2018**</u>

items.value: The Securities and Exchange Board of India ("SEBI" or "the Board") has been, from time to time, issuing various circulars/directions under the relevant provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018 (hereinafter referred to as "ICDR Regulations") (link).
items.md: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (**"SEBI"** or **"the Board"**) has been, from time to time, issuing various circulars/directions under the relevant provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018 (hereinafter referred to as **"ICDR Regulations"**) ([link](https://www.sebi.gov.in/sebiweb/home/HomeAction.do?doListing=yes&sid=1&ssid=3&smid=0)).

items.value: In order to enable the users to have access to the provisions of the applicable circulars at one place, a Master Circular for compliance with the provisions of the ICDR Regulations was issued on June 21, 2023. Further, the Master Circular dated June 21, 2023 was updated and issued on November 11, 2024 by consolidating relevant circulars issued till September 30, 2024.
items.md: In order to enable the users to have access to the provisions of the applicable circulars at one place, a Master Circular for compliance with the provisions of the ICDR Regulations was issued on June 21, 2023. Further, the Master Circular dated June 21, 2023 was updated and issued on November 11, 2024 by consolidating relevant circulars issued till September 30, 2024.

items.value: The Master Circular dated November 11, 2024 is updated to include all relevant circulars that were issued till December 31, 2025 and changes, wherever considered relevant, are carried out to reflect the provisions which are currently in force.
items.md: The Master Circular dated November 11, 2024 is updated to include all relevant circulars that were issued till December 31, 2025 and changes, wherever considered relevant, are carried out to reflect the provisions which are currently in force.

items.value: With the issuance of this Master Circular, all directions/instructions contained in the circulars listed out in the Appendix to this Master Circular shall stand rescinded to the extent they relate to ICDR Regulations.
items.md: With the issuance of this Master Circular, all directions/instructions contained in the circulars listed out in the Appendix to this Master Circular shall stand rescinded to the extent they relate to ICDR Regulations.

1. The Securities and Exchange Board of India (**"SEBI"** or **"the Board"**) has been, from time to time, issuing various circulars/directions under the relevant provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018 (hereinafter referred to as **"ICDR Regulations"**) ([link](https://www.sebi.gov.in/sebiweb/home/HomeAction.do?doListing=yes&sid=1&ssid=3&smid=0)).

2. In order to enable the users to have access to the provisions of the applicable circulars at one place, a Master Circular for compliance with the provisions of the ICDR Regulations was issued on June 21, 2023. Further, the Master Circular dated June 21, 2023 was updated and issued on November 11, 2024 by consolidating relevant circulars issued till September 30, 2024.

3. The Master Circular dated November 11, 2024 is updated to include all relevant circulars that were issued till December 31, 2025 and changes, wherever considered relevant, are carried out to reflect the provisions which are currently in force.

4. With the issuance of this Master Circular, all directions/instructions contained in the circulars listed out in the Appendix to this Master Circular shall stand rescinded to the extent they relate to ICDR Regulations.

5. Notwithstanding such rescission, -

items.value: (a) anything done or any action taken or purported to have been done or taken under the rescinded circulars, prior to such rescission, shall be deemed to have been done or taken under the corresponding provisions of this Master Circular;
items.md: (a) anything done or any action taken or purported to have been done or taken under the rescinded circulars, prior to such rescission, shall be deemed to have been done or taken under the corresponding provisions of this Master Circular;

items.value: (b) any application made to the Board under the rescinded circulars, prior to such rescission, and pending before it shall be deemed to have been made under the corresponding provisions of this Master Circular; and
items.md: (b) any application made to the Board under the rescinded circulars, prior to such rescission, and pending before it shall be deemed to have been made under the corresponding provisions of this Master Circular; and

items.value: (c) the previous operation of the rescinded circulars or anything duly done or suffered thereunder, any right, privilege, obligation or liability acquired, accrued or incurred under the rescinded circulars, any penalty, incurred in respect of any violation committed against the rescinded circulars, or any investigation, legal proceeding or remedy in respect of any such right, privilege, obligation, liability, penalty as aforesaid, shall remain unaffected as if the rescinded circulars have never been rescinded.
items.md: (c) the previous operation of the rescinded circulars or anything duly done or suffered thereunder, any right, privilege, obligation or liability acquired, accrued or incurred under the rescinded circulars, any penalty, incurred in respect of any violation committed against the rescinded circulars, or any investigation, legal proceeding or remedy in respect of any such right, privilege, obligation, liability, penalty as aforesaid, shall remain unaffected as if the rescinded circulars have never been rescinded.

items.value: This Master Circular is issued in exercise of the powers conferred under sections 11(1) of the Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992 Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992 (<u>link</u>), to protect the interests of investors in securities and to promote the development of, and to regulate, the securities market.

items.value: This Master Circular is available on SEBI website at <u>www.sebi.gov.in</u> in the path "Home>Legal>Master Circulars".

rows:
Delay in completion of a bonus issue:

(i) Within 15 days from the date of approval of the issue by its board of directors – in cases where shareholders’ approval for capitalization of profits or reserves for making the bonus issue is not required.

(ii) Within 2 months from the date of the meeting of its board of directors wherein the decision to announce bonus issue was taken subject to shareholders’ approval – in cases where issuer is required to seek shareholders’ approval for capitalization of profits or reserves for making the bonus issue.
₹20,000 per day of non-compliance till the date of compliance.
Listed entities not completing the conversion of convertible securities and allotting the shares, within 18 months from the date of allotment of convertible securities.

As per Schedule XIX - Para (2) of ICDR Regulations under heading Application for listing, it is stated that: “The issuer shall make an application for listing, from the date of allotment, within such period as may be specified by the Board from time to time, to one or more recognized stock exchange(s)”.

In regard to above, it is specified that Issuer shall make an application to the exchange/s for listing in case of further issue of equity shares from the date of allotment within 20 days (unless otherwise specified).
Schedule XIX – Listing of Securities on Stock Exchanges.
items.html:
Chapter 1: Non-compliance with certain provision of SEBI ICDR Regulations¹
S. No. Violation Regulation / Schedule Fine
1. Delay in completion of a bonus issue:

(i) Within 15 days from the date of approval of the issue by its board of directors – in cases where shareholders’ approval for capitalization of profits or reserves for making the bonus issue is not required.

(ii) Within 2 months from the date of the meeting of its board of directors wherein the decision to announce bonus issue was taken subject to shareholders’ approval – in cases where issuer is required to seek shareholders’ approval for capitalization of profits or reserves for making the bonus issue.		 295 (1) ₹20,000 per day of non-compliance till the date of compliance.
2. Listed entities not completing the conversion of convertible securities and allotting the shares, within 18 months from the date of allotment of convertible securities. 162 Same as above.
3. As per Schedule XIX - Para (2) of ICDR Regulations under heading Application for listing, it is stated that: “The issuer shall make an application for listing, from the date of allotment, within such period as may be specified by the Board from time to time, to one or more recognized stock exchange(s)”.

In regard to above, it is specified that Issuer shall make an application to the exchange/s for listing in case of further issue of equity shares from the date of allotment within 20 days (unless otherwise specified).		 Schedule XIX – Listing of Securities on Stock Exchanges. Same as above.

items.value: <u>Chapter 1: Non-compliance with certain provision of SEBI ICDR Regulations</u><sup>1</sup>
items.md: # <u>Chapter 1: Non-compliance with certain provision of SEBI ICDR Regulations</u><sup>1</sup>

items.value: Regulation 297 and 298 of ICDR Regulations, inter-alia, specify liability of a listed entity or any other person for contravention and actions which can be taken by the respective stock exchange, the revocation of such actions and consequences for failure to pay fine in the manner specified by SEBI.
items.md: Regulation 297 and 298 of ICDR Regulations, *inter-alia*, specify liability of a listed entity or any other person for contravention and actions which can be taken by the respective stock exchange, the revocation of such actions and consequences for failure to pay fine in the manner specified by SEBI.

items.value: In pursuance of the above, for non-compliance with certain provisions of the ICDR Regulations, stock exchanges shall impose fines on the listed entities, as under:
items.md: In pursuance of the above, for non-compliance with certain provisions of the ICDR Regulations, stock exchanges shall impose fines on the listed entities, as under:

1. Regulation 297 and 298 of ICDR Regulations, *inter-alia*, specify liability of a listed entity or any other person for contravention and actions which can be taken by the respective stock exchange, the revocation of such actions and consequences for failure to pay fine in the manner specified by SEBI.

2. In pursuance of the above, for non-compliance with certain provisions of the ICDR Regulations, stock exchanges shall impose fines on the listed entities, as under:

(ii) Within 2 months from the date of the meeting of its board of directors wherein the decision to announce bonus issue was taken subject to shareholders’ approval – in cases where issuer is required to seek shareholders’ approval for capitalization of profits or reserves for making the bonus issue.
₹20,000 per day of non-compliance till the date of compliance.
Listed entities not completing the conversion of convertible securities and allotting the shares, within 18 months from the date of allotment of convertible securities.

As per Schedule XIX - Para (2) of ICDR Regulations under heading Application for listing, it is stated that: “The issuer shall make an application for listing, from the date of allotment, within such period as may be specified by the Board from time to time, to one or more recognized stock exchange(s)”.
In regard to above, it is specified that Issuer shall make an application to the exchange/s for listing in case of further issue of equity shares from the date of allotment within 20 days (unless otherwise specified).
Schedule XIX – Listing of Securities on Stock Exchanges.

rows: Listed entities entities shall make an application for make an application for trading approval to the stock exchange/s within 7 working days from the date of grant of listing days from the date of grant of listing approval by the stock exchange/s. the stock exchange/s.

3. The amount of fine realized as per the above structure shall continue to be credited to the “Investor Protection Fund” of the concerned stock exchange. 4. The recognized stock exchange shall disseminate on their website the names of noncompliant listed entities that are liable to pay fine for non-compliance, the amount of fine imposed, details of fines received, etc. 5. The recognized stock exchange shall issue notices to the non-compliant listed entities to ensure compliance and collect fine as per para 2 within 15 days from the date of such notice. 6. If any non-compliant listed entity fails to pay the fine, the recognized stock exchange may initiate appropriate enforcement action, including prosecution in furtherance of Regulation 298 of ICDR Regulations. Bonus Issue Delays:

7. With respect to bonus issue delays, it is clarified that:
7.1 The approvals for the listing and trading of promoters’ bonus shares may be granted by the Stock Exchange, only after payment of the requisite fine by the listed entity. However, the approvals for the listing and trading of bonus shares allotted to persons other than the promoter(s) may be granted in the interest of the investors, subject to compliance with other requirements.
8. The Stock Exchanges may deviate from the abovementioned provisions, wherever the interest of the investors is not adversely affected, if found necessary, only after recording reasons in writing.

items.value: 1.3. <mark>[In terms of Regulation 85 of SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018, Rights Issues shall be completed within 23 working days from the date of Board of Directors of the Issuer approving the Rights Issue.</mark>

items.value: 1.7.1. <mark>Validation of application bids received for subscribing to the shares in Rights Issue and finalization of basis of allotment shall also be carried out by the Stock Exchanges and Depositories along with the Registrar to the issue.]</mark>
items.md: 1.7.1. <mark>Validation of application bids received for subscribing to the shares in Rights Issue and finalization of basis of allotment shall also be carried out by the Stock Exchanges and Depositories along with the Registrar to the issue.]</mark>

items.value: 1.8. <u>With respect to dematerialized Rights Entitlements (REs) –</u>
items.md: 1.8. <u>With respect to dematerialized Rights Entitlements (REs) –</u>

items.value: 1.8.1. In the letter of offer [***]⁴, the issuer shall disclose the process of credit of REs in the demat account and renunciation thereof.
items.md: 1.8.1. In the letter of offer \[\*\*\*]⁴, the issuer shall disclose the process of credit of REs in the demat account and renunciation thereof.

items.value: 1.8.2. The REs with a separate ISIN shall be credited to the demat account of eligible shareholders in dematerialized form before the date of opening of the issue, against the shares held by them as on the record date.
items.md: 1.8.2. The REs with a separate ISIN shall be credited to the demat account of eligible shareholders in dematerialized form before the date of opening of the issue, against the shares held by them as on the record date.

items.md: 1.8.3. Physical shareholders shall be required to provide their demat account details to Issuer / Registrar to the Issue for credit of REs not later than two working days prior to the issue closing date, such that credit of REs in their demat account takes place at least 1 day before the issue closing date.

items.value: 1.9. <u>Trading of dematerialized REs on stock exchange platform –</u>
items.md: 1.9. <u>Trading of dematerialized REs on stock exchange platform –</u>

items.value: 1.9.1. REs shall be traded on secondary market platform of stock exchanges, with T+1 rolling settlement, similar to the equity shares. Trading in REs on the secondary market platform of stock exchanges shall commence along with the opening of the issue and shall be closed at least three working days prior to the closure of the rights issue.
items.md: 1.9.1. REs shall be traded on secondary market platform of stock exchanges, with T+1 rolling settlement, similar to the equity shares. Trading in REs on the secondary market platform of stock exchanges shall commence along with the opening of the issue and shall be closed at least three working days prior to the closure of the rights issue.

items.value: 1.9.2. Investors holding REs in dematerialized mode shall be able to renounce their entitlements by trading on stock exchange platform or off-market transfer. Such trades will be settled by transferring dematerialized REs through depository mechanism, in the same manner as done for all other types of securities.
items.md: 1.9.2. Investors holding REs in dematerialized mode shall be able to renounce their entitlements by trading on stock exchange platform or off-market transfer. Such trades will be settled by transferring dematerialized REs through depository mechanism, in the same manner as done for all other types of securities.

items.value: 1.9.3. Payment mode - Application for a rights issue shall be made only through ASBA facility.
items.md: 1.9.3. Payment mode - Application for a rights issue shall be made only through ASBA facility.

items.value: 1.9.4. No withdrawal of application by any shareholder shall be permitted after the issue closing date.
items.md: 1.9.4. No withdrawal of application by any shareholder shall be permitted after the issue closing date.

items.md: # <u>Chapter 3: Disclosures in offer document<sup>5</sup></u>

items.value: 1. Disclosures in the abridged prospectus and front cover page of the offer document-
items.md: **1. Disclosures in the abridged prospectus and front cover page of the offer document-**

items.value: 1.1. Disclosures in the Abridged Prospectus
items.md: ## 1.1. Disclosures in the Abridged Prospectus

items.value: 1.1.1. In terms of Regulation 34(1) of the ICDR Regulations, abridged prospectus shall contain the disclosures as specified in Annexure I of Part E of Schedule VI of the said regulations.
items.md: 1.1.1. In terms of Regulation 34(1) of the ICDR Regulations, abridged prospectus shall contain the disclosures as specified in Annexure I of Part E of Schedule VI of the said regulations.

items.value: 1.1.2. In order to further simplify, provide greater clarity and consistency in the disclosures across various documents and to provide additional but critical information in the abridged prospectus, the format for disclosures in the abridged prospectus has been revised and is placed at <u>Annexure II</u>.
items.md: 1.1.2. In order to further simplify, provide greater clarity and consistency in the disclosures across various documents and to provide additional but critical information in the abridged prospectus, the format for disclosures in the abridged prospectus has been revised and is placed at <u>Annexure II</u>.

items.value: 1.2. Disclosures in the front cover page of the offer document
items.md: ## 1.2. Disclosures in the front cover page of the offer document

items.value: 1.2.1. Clause 1(a) of Part A - Schedule VI of the ICDR Regulations specifies information to be disclosed on the front outside cover page of offer document.
items.md: 1.2.1. Clause 1(a) of Part A - Schedule VI of the ICDR Regulations specifies information to be disclosed on the front outside cover page of offer document.

items.value: 1.2.2. With regard to above, a format for disclosure on front outside cover page shall be as per the format placed at <u>Annexure III</u>.

items.value: 1.3. General Instructions
items.md: ## 1.3. General Instructions

items.value: 1.3.1. While the disclosures in the abridged prospectus shall be as per <u>Annexure II</u> of this Master Circular instead of Annexure I of Part E of Schedule VI of the ICDR Regulations, the disclosure on front outside cover page shall be as per <u>Annexure III</u> of this Master Circular.
items.md: 1.3.1. items.value: 1.3.2. A copy of the abridged prospectus shall be made available on the website of issuer company, lead managers, registrar to an issuers and a link for downloading abridged prospectus shall be provided in price band advertisement.
items.md: 1.3.2. A copy of the abridged prospectus shall be made available on the website of issuer company, lead managers, registrar to an issuers and a link for downloading abridged prospectus shall be provided in price band advertisement.

items.value: 1.3.3. The Issuer Company / Merchant Bankers (MBs) shall ensure that the disclosures in the abridged prospectus are adequate, accurate and does not contain any misleading or misstatement.
items.md: 1.3.3. The Issuer Company / Merchant Bankers (MBs) shall ensure that the disclosures in the abridged prospectus are adequate, accurate and does not contain any misleading or misstatement.

items.value: 1.3.4. Also, no qualitative statement shall be made which cannot be substantiated with KPIs.

items.value: 3. Audiovisual (AV) presentation of disclosures made in Public Issue Offer Documents⁹
items.md: # 3. Audiovisual (AV) presentation of disclosures made in Public Issue Offer Documents⁹

items.value: 3.1 In order to create awareness among investors to not rely on unauthorized / unsolicited information about public issues, it was decided that salient disclosures made in the Draft Red Herring Prospectus (DRHP), Red Herring Prospects (RHP) and Price Band Advertisement for public issues shall also be made available in Audio Visual format (AV) for ease in understanding the features of public issues. Such AV shall be prepared and placed in the public domain for all main board public issues. The same shall initially be in bilingual format i.e. English and Hindi. The Hindi version shall contain text in Devanagari script.
items.md: 3.1 In order to create awareness among investors to not rely on unauthorized / unsolicited information about public issues, it was decided that salient disclosures made in the Draft Red Herring Prospectus (DRHP), Red Herring Prospects (RHP) and Price Band Advertisement for public issues shall also be made available in Audio Visual format (AV) for ease in understanding the features of public issues. Such AV shall be prepared and placed in the public domain for all main board public issues. The same shall initially be in bilingual format i.e. *English* and *Hindi*. The Hindi version shall contain text in Devanagari script.

items.value: 3.2 The contents of the AV shall be as per the following guidelines –
items.md: 3.2 The contents of the AV shall be as per the following guidelines –

items.value: 3.2.1 AV to be in compliance with the provisions regarding “Public communications and publicity materials” prescribed under Schedule IX of ICDR Regulations.
items.md: 3.2.1 AV to be in compliance with the provisions regarding “Public communications and publicity materials” prescribed under Schedule IX of ICDR Regulations.

items.value: 3.2.2 It shall contain the following disclosure to investors –
items.md: 3.2.2 It shall contain the following disclosure to investors –

items.value: “Investors are advised not to rely on any other document, content or information provided in respect to the public issue on the internet/ websites / social media platforms / micro-blogging platforms by finfluencers.
items.md: “*Investors are advised not to rely on any other document, content or information provided in respect to the public issue on the internet/ websites / social media platforms / micro-blogging platforms by finfluencers.*

items.value: Investors are advised to rely only on the information contained in the Offer document and Price Band Advertisement for making investment decision.”
items.md: *Investors are advised to rely only on the information contained in the Offer document and Price Band Advertisement for making investment decision.*”

items.value: 3.2.3 The duration of each bilingual version of the AV shall be approximately 10 minutes.
items.md: 3.2.3 The duration of each bilingual version of the AV shall be approximately 10 minutes.

items.value: 3.2.4 The total duration of the AV shall be equitably distributed to cover material disclosures made under various sections of the DRHP and RHP viz. about the company, risk factors, capital structure, objects of the offer, business of the issuer, promoters, management, summary of financial information, litigations, material developments and terms of the offer etc.
items.md: 3.2.4 The total duration of the AV shall be equitably distributed to cover material disclosures made under various sections of the DRHP and RHP viz. about the company, risk factors, capital structure, objects of the offer, business of the issuer, promoters, management, summary of financial information, litigations, material developments and terms of the offer etc.

items.value: 3.2.5 The content of the AV must be factual, non-repetitive, non-promotional and shall not be misleading in any manner.
items.md: 3.2.5 The content of the AV must be factual, non-repetitive, non-promotional and shall not be misleading in any manner.

items.value: 3.3 The AV shall be uploaded on the website of the Issuer and Association of Investment Bankers of India (AIBI) within 5 working days of the filing of DRHP with
items.md: 3.3 The AV shall be uploaded on the website of the Issuer and Association of Investment Bankers of India (AIBI) within 5 working days of the filing of DRHP with

items.value: 3.4 The AV shall also be made available on digital/social media platforms of the Issuer and AIBI. The web link of the said AV shall be made available on the websites of the Stock Exchanges and the Lead Managers to the issue. items.md: 3.4 The AV shall also be made available on digital/social media platforms of the Issuer and AIBI. The web link of the said AV shall be made available on the websites of the Stock Exchanges and the Lead Managers to the issue. The AV shall be made accessible through QR code (as made available in offer related documents) pertaining to the public issue.

items.value: 3.5 The AV shall be updated with information disclosed in RHP/ Prospectus and price band advertisement including details of the issue opening/closing date, price/ price-band etc., and uploaded on the date of publication of the price band advertisement or the date of filing of prospectus (in case of fixed price issues).
items.md: 3.5 The AV shall be updated with information disclosed in RHP/ Prospectus and price band advertisement including details of the issue opening/closing date, price/ price-band etc., and uploaded on the date of publication of the price band advertisement or the date of filing of prospectus (in case of fixed price issues).

items.value: 3.6 The Issuer and all Lead Managers to the public issue shall be responsible for the content and information made available in the AV.
items.md: 3.6 The Issuer and all Lead Managers to the public issue shall be responsible for the content and information made available in the AV.

items.value: 3.7 The above provisions with respect to AV presentation shall be applicable on voluntary basis to all DRHP filed with SEBI till September 30, 2024 and on mandatory basis to all DRHP filed with SEBI from October 01, 2024 onwards.
items.md: 3.7 The above provisions with respect to AV presentation shall be applicable on voluntary basis to all DRHP filed with SEBI till September 30, 2024 and on mandatory basis to all DRHP filed with SEBI from October 01, 2024 onwards.

items.value: <u>Chapter 5: Compensation to Retail Individual Investors (RIIs) in an IPO</u> <sup>10</sup>
items.md: # <u>Chapter 5: Compensation to Retail Individual Investors (RIIs) in an IPO</u> <sup>10</sup>

items.value: Applications Supported by Block Amount (ASBA) has resulted in almost complete elimination of complaints pertaining to refunds, there have been instances where the applicants in an Initial Public Offering have failed to get allotment of specified securities and in the process may have suffered an opportunity loss due to the following factors:
items.md: Applications Supported by Block Amount (ASBA) has resulted in almost complete elimination of complaints pertaining to refunds, there have been instances where the applicants in an Initial Public Offering have failed to get allotment of specified securities and in the process may have suffered an opportunity loss due to the following factors:

1. Applications Supported by Block Amount (ASBA) has resulted in almost complete elimination of complaints pertaining to refunds, there have been instances where the applicants in an Initial Public Offering have failed to get allotment of specified securities and in the process may have suffered an opportunity loss due to the following factors:

items.value: 1.1. Failure on part of the Self Certified Syndicate Banks (SCSB) to make bids in the concerned Exchange system even after the amount has been blocked in the investors' bank account with such SCSB.
items.md: 1.1. Failure on part of the Self Certified Syndicate Banks (SCSB) to make bids in the concerned Exchange system even after the amount has been blocked in the investors' bank account with such SCSB.

items.value: 1.2. Failure on part of the SCSB to process the ASBA applications even when they have been submitted within time.
items.md: 1.2. Failure on part of the SCSB to process the ASBA applications even when they have been submitted within time.

items.value: 1.3. Any other failures on part of an SCSB which has resulted in the rejection of the application form.
items.md: 1.3. Any other failures on part of an SCSB which has resulted in the rejection of the application form.

items.value: In order to have a uniform policy for calculation of minimum compensation payable to investors in scenarios as mentioned above in Para 1 a), b) and c), the following factors have been taken into account:
items.md: In order to have a uniform policy for calculation of minimum compensation payable to investors in scenarios as mentioned above in Para 1 a), b) and c), the following factors have been taken into account:

2. In order to have a uniform policy for calculation of minimum compensation payable to investors in scenarios as mentioned above in Para 1 a), b) and c), the following factors have been taken into account:

items.value: 2.1. the opportunity loss suffered by the investor due to non-allotment of shares;
items.md: 2.1. the opportunity loss suffered by the investor due to non-allotment of shares;

items.value: 2.2. the number of times the issue was oversubscribed in the relevant category;
items.md: 2.2. the number of times the issue was oversubscribed in the relevant category;

items.value: 2.3. the probability of allotment; and
items.md: 2.3. the probability of allotment; and

items.value: 2.4. the listing gains if any on the day of listing.
items.md: 2.4. the listing gains if any on the day of listing.

items.value: The following formula is mandated for calculation of minimum fair compensation:
items.md: The following formula is mandated for calculation of minimum fair compensation:
3. The following formula is mandated for calculation of minimum fair compensation:

items.value: <u>Compensation</u> = (Listing price* - Issue Price) X No. of shares that would have been allotted if bid was successful X Probability of allotment of shares determined on the basis of allotment
items.md: **<u>Compensation</u> = (*Listing price\** - Issue Price) X No. of shares that would have been allotted if bid was successful X Probability of allotment of shares determined on the basis of allotment**

items.value: *Listing price shall be taken as the highest of the opening prices on the day of listing across the recognized stock Exchanges.
items.md: \*Listing price shall be taken as the highest of the opening prices on the day of listing across the recognized stock Exchanges.

items.value: The formula is explained with the help of an example in the <u>Annexure IV</u> to this circular.
items.md: The formula is explained with the help of an example in the <u>Annexure IV</u> to this circular.

items.value: In case of issues which are subscribed between 90-100%, i.e. non oversubscribed issues, the applicants would be compensated for all the shares which they would have been allotted.
items.md: In case of issues which are subscribed between 90-100%, i.e. non oversubscribed issues, the applicants would be compensated for all the shares which they would have been allotted.

items.value: No compensation would be payable to the applicant in case the listing price is below the issue price.
items.md: No compensation would be payable to the applicant in case the listing price is below the issue price.

4. In case of issues which are subscribed between 90-100%, i.e. non oversubscribed issues, the applicants would be compensated for all the shares which they would have been allotted.

5. No compensation would be payable to the applicant in case the listing price is below the issue price.

items.value: <u>Chapter 6: Guidelines on issuance of non-convertible debt instruments along with warrants ('NCDs with Warrants') in terms of Chapter VI - Qualified Institutions Placement of SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018<sup>11</sup></u>
items.md: # <u>Chapter 6: Guidelines on issuance of non-convertible debt instruments along with warrants ('NCDs with Warrants') in terms of Chapter VI - Qualified Institutions Placement of SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018<sup>11</sup></u>

items.value: In order to streamline procedure of issuance and applicability of Electronic Book Provider Platform (EBP Platform) mechanism on the 'NCDs portion' the following was decided and made applicable for issues wherein the size of NCDs portion is above threshold prescribed under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Non-Convertible Securities) Regulations, 2021 ("NCS Regulations 2021") and Circulars issued there under:
items.md: In order to streamline procedure of issuance and applicability of Electronic Book Provider Platform (EBP Platform) mechanism on the 'NCDs portion' the following was decided and made applicable for issues wherein the size of NCDs portion is above threshold prescribed under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Non-Convertible Securities) Regulations, 2021 (**"NCS Regulations 2021"**) and Circulars issued there under:

1. In order to streamline procedure of issuance and applicability of Electronic Book Provider Platform (EBP Platform) mechanism on the 'NCDs portion' the following was decided and made applicable for issues wherein the size of NCDs portion is above threshold prescribed under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Non-Convertible Securities) Regulations, 2021 (**"NCS Regulations 2021"**) and Circulars issued there under:

items.value: 1.1. EBP platform mechanism shall be mandatory for 'NCDs portion' of the issue (for both stapled and segregated offer) and issuer shall be required to comply with the NCS Regulations 2021, and Circulars issued there under.
items.md: 1.1. EBP platform mechanism shall be mandatory for 'NCDs portion' of the issue (for both stapled and segregated offer) and issuer shall be required to comply with the NCS Regulations 2021, and Circulars issued there under.

items.value: 1.2. 'Warrants portion' of the issue shall be in terms of Chapter VI on Qualified Institutions Placement under the ICDR Regulations 2018.
items.md: 1.2. 'Warrants portion' of the issue shall be in terms of Chapter VI on Qualified Institutions Placement under the ICDR Regulations 2018.

items.value: 1.3. Of the 'total issue size' of the issue, at least 40% size shall consist of 'Warrants portion'. It may be noted that 'total issue size' shall mean combined size of NCDs issue and the aggregate size of the warrants portion, including the conversion price of warrants.
items.md: 1.3. Of the 'total issue size' of the issue, at least 40% size shall consist of 'Warrants portion'. It may be noted that 'total issue size' shall mean combined size of NCDs issue and the aggregate size of the warrants portion, including the conversion price of warrants.

items.value: The segregated offer of NCDs and stapled offer, both are exempted from the requirements as prescribed under the Regulations 175(3), 179(2)(a), 180(1) and 180(2) of the ICDR Regulations.
items.md: The segregated offer of NCDs and stapled offer, both are exempted from the requirements as prescribed under the Regulations 175(3), 179(2)(a), 180(1) and 180(2) of the ICDR Regulations.
2. The segregated offer of NCDs and stapled offer, both are exempted from the requirements as prescribed under the Regulations 175(3), 179(2)(a), 180(1) and 180(2) of the ICDR Regulations.

items.value: <u>Chapter 7: Framework for the process of recognition of investors for the purpose of Innovators Growth Platform<sup>12</sup></u>
items.md: # <u>Chapter 7: Framework for the process of recognition of investors for the purpose of Innovators Growth Platform<sup>12</sup></u>

items.value: Innovators Growth Platform Investors ("IGPIs") for the limited purpose of Innovators Growth Platform ("IGP"), are investors whose holding in the Issuer Company, is eligible for the computation of at least 25% of the pre-issue capital in accordance with Regulation 283(1) of the ICDR Regulations.
items.md: Innovators Growth Platform Investors ("IGPIs") for the limited purpose of Innovators Growth Platform ("IGP"), are investors whose holding in the Issuer Company, is eligible for the computation of at least 25% of the pre-issue capital in accordance with Regulation 283(1) of the ICDR Regulations.

items.value: Accordingly, the framework for the process of recognition of investors as IGPI is detailed below:
items.md: Accordingly, the framework for the process of recognition of investors as IGPI is detailed below:

1. Innovators Growth Platform Investors ("IGPIs") for the limited purpose of Innovators Growth Platform ("IGP"), are investors whose holding in the Issuer Company, is eligible for the computation of at least 25% of the pre-issue capital in accordance with Regulation 283(1) of the ICDR Regulations.

2. Accordingly, the framework for the process of recognition of investors as IGPI is detailed below:

items.value: <u>A. Eligibility</u>
items.md: <u>A. Eligibility</u>

items.value: The following entities shall be eligible to be considered as IGPIs:
items.md: The following entities shall be eligible to be considered as IGPIs:

items.value: i. An individual with total gross income of ₹50 lakhs annually and who has minimum liquid net worth of ₹5 crores; or
items.md: i. An individual with total gross income of ₹50 lakhs annually and who has minimum liquid net worth of ₹5 crores; or

items.value: ii. A body corporate with net worth of ₹25 crores
items.md: ii. A body corporate with net worth of ₹25 crores

items.value: <u>B. Procedure for Recognition</u>
items.md: <u>B. Procedure for Recognition</u>

items.value: i. Responsibility of Stock Exchanges/Depositories
items.md: i. Responsibility of Stock Exchanges/Depositories

items.value: The investor, having a demat account with a Depository, will make an application to the Stock Exchanges/Depositories in the manner prescribed by them for recognition as an IGPI. The Stock Exchanges /Depositories may use the services of Brokers/Depository participants respectively for such purpose. However, Stock Exchanges/Depositories shall be responsible for verification and maintenance of the IGPI data.
items.md: The investor, having a demat account with a Depository, will make an application to the Stock Exchanges/Depositories in the manner prescribed by them for recognition as an IGPI. The Stock Exchanges /Depositories may use the services of Brokers/Depository participants respectively for such purpose. However, Stock Exchanges/Depositories shall be responsible for verification and maintenance of the IGPI data.

items.value: ii. Documentation
items.md: ii. Documentation

items.value: Detailed Documentation required for recognition is provided at <u>Annexure V</u> of this master Circular.
items.md: Detailed Documentation required for recognition is provided at <u>Annexure V</u> of this master Circular.

items.value: 3. <u>Validity of Recognition</u>
items.md: ### 3. <u>Validity of Recognition</u>

items.value: The recognition granted by the Stock Exchange/Depository shall be valid for a period of three years from the date of issue of such accreditation unless the IGPI becomes ineligible due to change in his/her/its financial status in which case such IGPI shall inform the Stock Exchange/Depository of such ineligibility.
items.md: The recognition granted by the Stock Exchange/Depository shall be valid for a period of three years from the date of issue of such accreditation unless the IGPI becomes ineligible due to change in his/her/its financial status in which case such IGPI shall inform the Stock Exchange/Depository of such ineligibility.

items.value: <u>Responsibility of Merchant Bankers at the time of listing on IGP with regard to IGPIs</u>
items.md: <u>Responsibility of Merchant Bankers at the time of listing on IGP with regard to IGPIs</u>
4. <u>Responsibility of Merchant Bankers at the time of listing on IGP with regard to IGPIs</u>

items.value: At the time of application by a Company for listing on IGP, the merchant bankers shall ensure due diligence with regard to eligibility of IGPIs and that their holding in the Company desirous of listing on IGP is in accordance with the Regulation 283(1) of the ICDR Regulations 2018.
items.md: At the time of application by a Company for listing on IGP, the merchant bankers shall ensure due diligence with regard to eligibility of IGPIs and that their holding in the Company desirous of listing on IGP is in accordance with the Regulation 283(1) of the ICDR Regulations 2018.

items.value: <u>Chapter 8: Issue Summary Document (ISD) and dissemination of issue advertisements</u><sup>13</sup>
items.md: # <u>Chapter 8: Issue Summary Document (ISD) and dissemination of issue advertisements</u><sup>13</sup>

items.value: In order to facilitate consumption of data by stakeholders such as researchers, policy makers, market analysts, and market participants, in respect of public issues, further issues, buyback, offers under SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011 (“SEBI SAST Regulations”) and SEBI (Delisting of Equity Shares) Regulations, 2021 (“SEBI Delisting Regulations”), etc., it was decided to make available relevant information / data points at the Stock Exchanges and Depositories in a structured manner.
items.md: In order to facilitate consumption of data by stakeholders such as researchers, policy makers, market analysts, and market participants, in respect of public issues, further issues, buyback, offers under SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011 (“**SEBI SAST Regulations**”) and SEBI (Delisting of Equity Shares) Regulations, 2021 (“**SEBI Delisting Regulations**”), etc., it was decided to make available relevant information / data points at the Stock Exchanges and Depositories in a structured manner.

items.value: Accordingly, after consultation with the stakeholders, an Issue Summary Document (ISD) was designed. It was decided to introduce the ISD for the following, in XBRL (Extensible Business Reporting Language) format:
items.md: Accordingly, after consultation with the stakeholders, an Issue Summary Document (ISD) was designed. It was decided to introduce the ISD for the following, in XBRL (Extensible Business Reporting Language) format:

1. In order to facilitate consumption of data by stakeholders such as researchers, policy makers, market analysts, and market participants, in respect of public issues, further issues, buyback, offers under SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011 (“**SEBI SAST Regulations**”) and SEBI (Delisting of Equity Shares) Regulations, 2021 (“**SEBI Delisting Regulations**”), etc., it was decided to make available relevant information / data points at the Stock Exchanges and Depositories in a structured manner.

2. Accordingly, after consultation with the stakeholders, an Issue Summary Document (ISD) was designed. It was decided to introduce the ISD for the following, in XBRL (Extensible Business Reporting Language) format:

items.value: 2.1 public issue of specified securities (initial public offer / further public offer);
items.md: 2.1 public issue of specified securities (initial public offer / further public offer);

items.value: 2.2 further issues {preferential issue, qualified institutions placement (QIP), rights issue, issue of American Depository Receipts (ADR), Global Depository Receipts (GDR) and Foreign Currency Convertible Bonds (FCCBs)};
items.md: 2.2 further issues {preferential issue, qualified institutions placement (QIP), rights issue, issue of American Depository Receipts (ADR), Global Depository Receipts (GDR) and Foreign Currency Convertible Bonds (FCCBs)};

items.value: 2.3 buy-back of equity shares (through tender offer or from the open market);
items.md: 2.3 buy-back of equity shares (through tender offer or from the open market);

items.value: 2.4 open offer under SEBI SAST Regulations;
items.md: 2.4 open offer under SEBI SAST Regulations;

items.value: 2.5 voluntary delisting of equity shares where exit opportunity is required under SEBI Delisting Regulations.
items.md: 2.5 voluntary delisting of equity shares where exit opportunity is required under SEBI Delisting Regulations.

items.value: ISD shall be filed in two stages:
items.md: ISD shall be filed in two stages:
3. ISD shall be filed in two stages:

items.value: 3.1 In the first stage, ISD will be filed containing pre-issue / offer fields.
items.md: 3.1 In the first stage, ISD will be filed containing pre-issue / offer fields.

items.value: 3.2 In the second stage, ISD will be filed containing post-issue / offer fields after allotment/offer is completed / as applicable for respective ISD.
items.md: 3.2 In the second stage, ISD will be filed containing post-issue / offer fields after allotment/offer is completed / as applicable for respective ISD.

items.value: The formats for ISD are placed as Table I to Table X in <u>Annexure VI</u>:
items.md: The formats for ISD are placed as Table I to Table X in <u>Annexure VI</u>:
4. The formats for ISD are placed as Table I to Table X in <u>Annexure VI</u>:

items.value: 4.1 Table I: ISD for public issue of specified securities
items.md: 4.1 Table I: ISD for public issue of specified securities

items.md: 4.2 Table II: ISD for preferential issue of specified securities

items.value: The prescribed formats also provide timeline for submission of the details and also casts responsibility on the entity responsible for the submission (hereinafter referred to as “Submitting Entity”).
items.md: The prescribed formats also provide timeline for submission of the details and also casts responsibility on the entity responsible for the submission (hereinafter referred to as “Submitting Entity”).

items.value: Stock Exchanges shall develop a utility in order to facilitate the filing of the ISD by Submitting Entity. The Submitting Entity may file the details, as applicable in the format mentioned at para 4 above, to any stock exchange where the securities of the entity, in relation to which the ISD is being filed, are listed / proposed to be listed.
items.md: Stock Exchanges shall develop a utility in order to facilitate the filing of the ISD by Submitting Entity. The Submitting Entity may file the details, as applicable in the format mentioned at para 4 above, to any stock exchange where the securities of the entity, in relation to which the ISD is being filed, are listed / proposed to be listed.

items.value: The Stock Exchange which receives the ISD shall further transmit, as soon as possible, the information to other Stock Exchanges and Depositories for dissemination.
items.md: The Stock Exchange which receives the ISD shall further transmit, as soon as possible, the information to other Stock Exchanges and Depositories for dissemination.

items.value: Dissemination on the websites of Stock Exchanges and Depositories:
items.md: Dissemination on the websites of Stock Exchanges and Depositories:

5. The prescribed formats also provide timeline for submission of the details and also casts responsibility on the entity responsible for the submission (hereinafter referred to as “Submitting Entity”).

6. Stock Exchanges shall develop a utility in order to facilitate the filing of the ISD by Submitting Entity. The Submitting Entity may file the details, as applicable in the format mentioned at para 4 above, to any stock exchange where the securities of the entity, in relation to which the ISD is being filed, are listed / proposed to be listed.

7. The Stock Exchange which receives the ISD shall further transmit, as soon as possible, the information to other Stock Exchanges and Depositories for dissemination.

8. Dissemination on the websites of Stock Exchanges and Depositories:

items.value: 8.1 ISD should be disseminated through websites of the Recognised Stock Exchanges (where the entity is listed / is proposed to be listed) and the Depositories.
items.md: 8.1 ISD should be disseminated through websites of the Recognised Stock Exchanges (where the entity is listed / is proposed to be listed) and the Depositories.

items.value: 8.2 All entities involved in the process are advised to take necessary steps to ensure compliance in this regard.
items.md: 8.2 All entities involved in the process are advised to take necessary steps to ensure compliance in this regard.

items.value: Implementation in phases:
items.md: Implementation in phases:
9. Implementation in phases:

items.value: 9.1 In the first phase, the roll-out took place of ISD for public issues of specified securities, for offer documents filed on or after March 01, 2023.
items.md: 9.1 In the first phase, the roll-out took place of ISD for public issues of specified securities, for offer documents filed on or after March 01, 2023.

items.value: 9.2 In the second phase, ISD for further issues was implemented from April 03, 2023.
items.md: 9.2 In the second phase, ISD for further issues was implemented from April 03, 2023.

items.value: 9.3 In the third phase, ISD for open offer, buy-back and voluntary delisting shall be implemented from May 02, 2023.
items.md: 9.3 In the third phase, ISD for open offer, buy-back and voluntary delisting shall be implemented from May 02, 2023.

items.value: Further, Lead Managers are required to disseminate all advertisements in connection with a public issue under ICDR Regulations 2018 in pdf format on the website of the stock exchange(s) from March 01, 2023.
items.md: Further, Lead Managers are required to disseminate all advertisements in connection with a public issue under ICDR Regulations 2018 **in pdf format** on the website of the stock exchange(s) from March 01, 2023.

items.value: The recognized Stock Exchanges and the Depositories are directed to:
items.md: The recognized Stock Exchanges and the Depositories are directed to:

10. Further, Lead Managers are required to disseminate all advertisements in connection with a public issue under ICDR Regulations 2018 **in pdf format** on the website of the stock exchange(s) from March 01, 2023.

11. The recognized Stock Exchanges and the Depositories are directed to:

items.value: 11.1. bring the provisions of this chapter to the notice of all registered merchant bankers, listed entities and issuers and also disseminate the same on their websites; and
items.md: 11.1. bring the provisions of this chapter to the notice of all registered merchant bankers, listed entities and issuers and also disseminate the same on their websites; and

items.value: 11.2. communicate to SEBI, the status of implementation of the provisions of this chapter within 7 days of implementation and in their Monthly Developments Reports.
items.md: 11.2. communicate to SEBI, the status of implementation of the provisions of this chapter within 7 days of implementation and in their Monthly Developments Reports.

items.value: <u>Chapter 9: Applications Supported by Blocked Amount (ASBA) Facility</u>
items.md: # <u>Chapter 9: Applications Supported by Blocked Amount (ASBA) Facility</u>

items.value: SEBI, in its continuing endeavor to provide an efficient mechanism for raising funds, has been continuously striving to streamline the process and methodologies associated with public issue and rights issue fund raising process. Towards this end, SEBI introduced Application Supported by Blocked Amount (ASBA) facility on September 01, 2008<sup>14</sup>.
items.md: SEBI, in its continuing endeavor to provide an efficient mechanism for raising funds, has been continuously striving to streamline the process and methodologies associated with public issue and rights issue fund raising process. Towards this end, SEBI introduced Application Supported by Blocked Amount (ASBA) facility on September 01, 2008<sup>14</sup>.

items.value: Definition of ASBA<sup>15</sup>: ASBA means ‘Application Supported by Blocked Amount’ as defined in clause (d) of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 2 of the ICDR Regulations 2018 i.e.
items.md: **Definition of ASBA<sup>15</sup>**: ASBA means ‘Application Supported by Blocked Amount’ as defined in clause (d) of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 2 of the ICDR Regulations 2018 i.e.
1. **Definition of ASBA<sup>15</sup>**: ASBA means ‘Application Supported by Blocked Amount’ as defined in clause (d) of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 2 of the ICDR Regulations 2018 i.e.

items.value: “application supported by blocked amount (ASBA) means an application for subscribing to a public issue or rights issue, along with an authorisation to self-certified syndicate bank to block the application money in a bank account”
items.md: “application supported by blocked amount (ASBA) means an application for subscribing to a public issue or rights issue, along with an authorisation to self-certified syndicate bank to block the application money in a bank account”

items.value: Applicability of ASBA<sup>16</sup>: ASBA process is applicable in case of both Public Issue and Rights Issue. All investors (Retail, QIBs, NIIs etc.) / shareholders are eligible to apply through ASBA. All the investors / shareholders making application in public issue / rights issue shall use ASBA facility for making payment i.e. mention their bank account number and authorise the bank to make payment in case of allotment by signing the application forms, thus obviating the need of writing the cheques. In this regard, disclosures shall be made in the offer document such as in issue procedure section as part of payment instructions.
items.md: **Applicability of ASBA<sup>16</sup>**: ASBA process is applicable in case of both Public Issue and Rights Issue. All investors (Retail, QIBs, NIIs etc.) / shareholders are eligible to apply through ASBA. All the investors / shareholders making application in public issue / rights issue shall use ASBA facility for making payment i.e. mention their bank account number and authorise the bank to make payment in case of allotment by signing the application forms, thus obviating the need of writing the cheques. In this regard, disclosures shall be made in the offer document such as in issue procedure section as part of payment instructions.

items.md: **SCSBs in ASBA<sup>17</sup>**

2. **Applicability of ASBA<sup>16</sup>**: ASBA process is applicable in case of both Public Issue and Rights Issue. All investors (Retail, QIBs, NIIs etc.) / shareholders are eligible to apply through ASBA. All the investors / shareholders making application in public issue / rights issue shall use ASBA facility for making payment i.e. mention their bank account number and authorise the bank to make payment in case of allotment by signing the application forms, thus obviating the need of writing the cheques. In this regard, disclosures shall be made in the offer document such as in issue procedure section as part of payment instructions.

items.value: 3.3. SCSBs are advised to ensure that for ASBA application, the SCSBs shall block the application amount only against / in a funded deposit account and ensure that clear demarcated funds are available for ASBA applications.<sup>19</sup>
items.md: 3.3. SCSBs are advised to ensure that for ASBA application, the SCSBs shall block the application amount only against / in a funded deposit account and ensure that clear demarcated funds are available for ASBA applications.<sup>19</sup>

items.value: 3.4. SCSBs are also advised to ensure that for making application by banks on own account using ASBA facility, SCSBs should have a separate account in own name with any other SEBI registered SCSBs. Such account shall be used solely for the purpose of making application in public issues and clear demarcated funds should be available in such account for ASBA applications.<sup>20</sup>
items.md: 3.4. SCSBs are also advised to ensure that for making application by banks on own account using ASBA facility, SCSBs should have a separate account in own name with any other SEBI registered SCSBs. Such account shall be used solely for the purpose of making application in public issues and clear demarcated funds should be available in such account for ASBA applications.<sup>20</sup>

items.value: 3.5. An SCSB shall identify its Designated Branches (DBs) at which an investor shall submit ASBA and shall also identify the Controlling Branch (CB) which shall act as a coordinating branch for the Registrar to the Issue, Stock Exchanges and Merchant Bankers. The SCSB, its DBs and CB shall continue to act as such, for all public and rights issues to which ASBA facility is applicable. The SCSB shall communicate the following details to Stock Exchanges for making it available on their respective websites. These details shall also be made available by the SCSB on its website:<sup>21</sup>
items.md: 3.5. The SCSB shall communicate the following details to Stock Exchanges for making it available on their respective websites. These details shall also be made available by the SCSB on its website:<sup>21</sup>

items.value: 3.5.3. Name and contact details of a nodal officer at a senior level from the CB.

items.value: 4. Entities Eligible for Accepting ASBA Application<sup>24</sup>:
items.md: ## 4. Entities Eligible for Accepting ASBA Application<sup>24</sup>:

items.value: 4.1. The following entities shall accept a completed Application-Cum-Bid form from investors intending to subscribe to a Public Issue:<sup>25</sup>
items.md: 4.1. The following entities shall accept a completed Application-Cum-Bid form from investors intending to subscribe to a Public Issue:<sup>25</sup>

items.value: 4.1.1. an SCSB, with whom the bank account to be blocked, is maintained,
items.md: 4.1.1. an SCSB, with whom the bank account to be blocked, is maintained,

items.value: 4.1.2. a syndicate member (or sub-syndicate member),
items.md: 4.1.2. a syndicate member (or sub-syndicate member),

items.value: 4.1.3. a stock broker registered with a recognised stock exchange (and whose name is maintained on the website of the stock exchange as eligible for this activity) ('broker'). The details of submission of ASBA through a stock broker are specified at Annexures VIII<sup>26</sup>.
items.md: 4.1.3. a stock broker registered with a recognised stock exchange (and whose name is maintained on the website of the stock exchange as eligible for this activity) ('broker'). The details of submission of ASBA through a stock broker are specified at Annexures VIII<sup>26</sup>.

items.value: 4.1.4. a depository participant ('DP') (whose name is mentioned on the website of the stock exchange as eligible for this activity),
items.md: 4.1.4. a depository participant ('DP') (whose name is mentioned on the website of the stock exchange as eligible for this activity),

items.value: 4.1.5. a register to an issue and share transfer agent ('RTA') (whose name is mentioned on the website of the stock exchange as eligible for this activity);
items.md: 4.1.5. a register to an issue and share transfer agent ('RTA') (whose name is mentioned on the website of the stock exchange as eligible for this activity);

items.value: 4.2. Retails investors submitting application with any of the entities at 4.1.2 to 4.1.5 above (hereinafter referred as 'Intermediaries'), have to use UPI and shall also mention their UPI ID in the Application-cum-bid form.<sup>27</sup>
items.md: 4.2. Retails investors submitting application with any of the entities at 4.1.2 to 4.1.5 above (hereinafter referred as 'Intermediaries'), have to use UPI and shall also mention their UPI ID in the Application-cum-bid form.<sup>27</sup>

items.value: 4.3. The RTAs and DPs shall provide their contact details, where the application forms shall be collected by them, to the recognized stock exchange as per the format specified at Annexure IX and the same shall be disclosed by the stock exchanges on their websites. RTAs and DPs shall regularly update the said details by furnishing current information to the Stock Exchanges which shall be disclosed by the Stock Exchanges.<sup>28</sup>
items.md: 4.3. The RTAs and DPs shall provide their contact details, where the application forms shall be collected by them, to the recognized stock exchange as per the format specified at Annexure IX and the same shall be disclosed by the stock exchanges on their websites. RTAs and DPs shall regularly update the said details by furnishing current information to the Stock Exchanges which shall be disclosed by the Stock Exchanges.<sup>28</sup>

items.value: 4.4. To facilitate Intermediaries to accept ASBA forms from investors, all the SCSBs, having a branch in the location of broker centers of Stock Exchanges, are required to name at least one branch where Intermediaries can submit the ASBA forms. The Stock Exchanges shall ensure that the details of the locations of their broker centers are disclosed on their websites and regularly updated.<sup>29</sup>
items.md: 4.4. items.value: 5. ASBA Process in Public Issue and Rights Issue<sup>30</sup>
items.md: ## 5. ASBA Process in Public Issue and Rights Issue<sup>30</sup>

items.value: 5.1. In Public Issue: An investor shall submit an ASBA physically or electronically to the SCSB (with whom the bank account to be blocked is maintained) or intermediaries mentioned above. The ASBA application in Public Issues shall be processed only after the application monies are blocked in the investor’s account.<sup>31</sup>
items.md: 5.1. **In Public Issue:** An investor shall submit an ASBA physically or electronically to the SCSB (with whom the bank account to be blocked is maintained) or intermediaries mentioned above. The ASBA application in Public Issues shall be processed only after the application monies are blocked in the investor’s account.<sup>31</sup>

items.value: 5.2. Processing of Applications by Intermediaries: Intermediaries accepting the application forms shall be responsible for uploading the bid along with other relevant details in application forms on the electronic bidding system of Stock Exchanges and submitting the form, wherever applicable, to SCSBs for blocking of funds (except in case of SCSBs, where blocking of funds will be done by respective SCSBs only). All applications shall be stamped and thereby acknowledged by the intermediary/SCSBs at the time of receipt.<sup>32</sup>
items.md: 5.2. **Processing of Applications by Intermediaries:** Intermediaries accepting the application forms shall be responsible for uploading the bid along with other relevant details in application forms on the electronic bidding system of Stock Exchanges and submitting the form, wherever applicable, to SCSBs for blocking of funds (except in case of SCSBs, where blocking of funds will be done by respective SCSBs only). All applications shall be stamped and thereby acknowledged by the intermediary/SCSBs at the time of receipt.<sup>32</sup>

items.value: 5.3. Processing of Applications by SCSBs: In case an ASBA application is directly submitted to SCSBs then SCSBs shall block the application money in the bank account specified in the ASBA, on the basis of an authorisation to this effect given by the account holder in the ASBA. The application money shall remain blocked in the bank account till finalisation of the basis of allotment of the issue or till withdrawal/ failure of the issue or till withdrawal / rejection of the application, as the case may be. The application data shall thereafter be uploaded by the SCSBs in the electronic bidding system through a web-enabled interface provided by the Stock Exchanges.<sup>33</sup>
items.md: 5.3. **Processing of Applications by SCSBs:** In case an ASBA application is directly submitted to SCSBs then SCSBs shall block the application money in the bank account specified in the ASBA, on the basis of an authorisation to this effect given by the account holder in the ASBA. The application money shall remain blocked in the bank account till finalisation of the basis of allotment of the issue or till withdrawal/ failure of the issue or till withdrawal / rejection of the application, as the case may be. The application data shall thereafter be uploaded by the SCSBs in the electronic bidding system through a web-enabled interface provided by the Stock Exchanges.<sup>33</sup>

items.value: 5.4. Stock Exchange(s) may validate the electronic bid details with depository’s records for DP ID, Client ID and PAN, at periodic intervals throughout the bidding day during the bidding period and bring the inconsistencies to the notice of intermediaries concerned, for rectification and re-submission within the time specified by Stock Exchange.<sup>34</sup>
items.md: 5.4. Stock Exchange(s) may validate the electronic bid details with depository’s records for DP ID, Client ID and PAN, at periodic intervals throughout the bidding day during the bidding period and bring the inconsistencies to the notice of intermediaries concerned, for rectification and re-submission within the time specified by Stock Exchange.<sup>34</sup>

items.value: 5.5. Once the basis of allotment is finalized, the Registrar to the Issue shall send an appropriate request to the CB of SCSB for unblocking the relevant bank accounts and for transferring the requisite amount to the issuer’s account designated for this purpose. In case of withdrawal/ failure of the issue, the amount shall be unblocked by
items.md: 5.5. items.value: 5.6. In Rights Issue: ASBA facility in rights issue enables an investor / shareholder to apply through ASBA mode. ASBA process from the time of submission of application by the applicants till transfer of shares in the depository account of the investors, as specified for public issues, shall be followed in the case of rights issues also to the extent relevant for rights issue. The role and responsibilities of SCSBs, Stock Exchanges and RTAs [**]<sup>36</sup> for public issues, shall be applicable mutatis mutandis to rights issue also.
items.md: 5.6. **In Rights Issue:** ASBA facility in rights issue enables an investor / shareholder to apply through ASBA mode. ASBA process from the time of submission of application by the applicants till transfer of shares in the depository account of the investors, as specified for public issues, shall be followed in the case of rights issues also to the extent relevant for rights issue. The role and responsibilities of SCSBs, Stock Exchanges and RTAs \[\*\*]<sup>36</sup> for public issues, shall be applicable mutatis mutandis to rights issue also.

items.value: 6. Application-cum-Bidding Form and Manner of Disclosure<sup>37</sup>
items.md: # 6. Application-cum-Bidding Form and Manner of Disclosure<sup>37</sup>

items.value: 6.1. There shall be a single standardized Application-Cum-Bidding-Form for investors. The full text of data fields required in Application-Cum-Bidding-Form is given at <u>Annexure X</u><sup>38</sup> and an illustrative format for Application-Cum-Bidding-Form is given at <u>Annexure XI</u><sup>39</sup>. A gist of major changes is given below:<sup>40</sup>
items.md: 6.1. There shall be a single standardized Application-Cum-Bidding-Form for investors. The full text of data fields required in Application-Cum-Bidding-Form is given at <u>Annexure X</u><sup>38</sup> and an illustrative format for Application-Cum-Bidding-Form is given at <u>Annexure XI</u><sup>39</sup>. A gist of major changes is given below:<sup>40</sup>

items.value: 6.1.1. the placement of the data fields has been done keeping in mind the ease of reference to investors/intermediaries for identification of name of the issue.
items.md: 6.1.1. the placement of the data fields has been done keeping in mind the ease of reference to investors/intermediaries for identification of name of the issue.

items.value: 6.1.2. company specific information is now included in abridged prospectus.
items.md: 6.1.2. company specific information is now included in abridged prospectus.

items.value: 6.1.3. information / instruction(s) which are required to fill the form have been placed overleaf of the Application Form. Remaining information / instruction(s) which are generic in nature have been incorporated in General Information Document
items.md: 6.1.3. information / instruction(s) which are required to fill the form have been placed overleaf of the Application Form. Remaining information / instruction(s) which are generic in nature have been incorporated in General Information Document

items.value: 6.1.4. the pre-printing of prefix number in the Application Form has been done away with to reduce errors caused in data entry. All forms shall henceforth carry eight digit application numbers which shall be serially numbered. Serial number may be system generated or stamped with automatic numbering machine and the forms should be date and time stamped at the bidding centers. In order to ensure correct categorization of bids, special series shall be used for employees (where employee reservation is applicable).

items.value: 7. Role and Responsibilities of Intermediaries in ASBA Facility<sup>46</sup>
items.md: # 7. Role and Responsibilities of Intermediaries in ASBA Facility<sup>46</sup>

items.value: 7.1. The details of the role and responsibilities of Intermediaries such as SCSBs, Registrars, Merchant Bankers and Stock Exchanges in the ASBA facility are given in <u>Annexure XII</u>, for due compliance by the respective intermediary.
items.md: 7.1. The details of the role and responsibilities of Intermediaries such as SCSBs, Registrars, Merchant Bankers and Stock Exchanges in the ASBA facility are given in <u>Annexure XII</u>, for due compliance by the respective intermediary.

items.value: 8. Payment of Commission and Processing Fee
items.md: # 8. Payment of Commission and Processing Fee

items.value: 8.1. In Public Issues, Intermediaries are entitled for selling commission for ASBA forms procured by them. SCSBs shall be entitled for per form processing fee for ASBA forms procured by Intermediaries and submitted with SCSBs. For ASBA forms procured directly by SCSBs, they would be entitled for selling commission only and no processing fee shall be paid to them.<sup>47</sup>
items.md: 8.1. In Public Issues, Intermediaries are entitled for selling commission for ASBA forms procured by them. SCSBs shall be entitled for per form processing fee for ASBA forms procured by Intermediaries and submitted with SCSBs. For ASBA forms procured directly by SCSBs, they would be entitled for selling commission only and no processing fee shall be paid to them.<sup>47</sup>

items.value: 8.2. Amount of commission payable to Intermediaries shall be determined on the basis of application which have been considered eligible for the purpose of allotment. In order to determine to which Intermediary the commission is payable to, the terminal from which the bid has been uploaded will be taken into account.<sup>48</sup>
items.md: 8.2. Amount of commission payable to Intermediaries shall be determined on the basis of application which have been considered eligible for the purpose of allotment. In order to determine to which Intermediary the commission is payable to, the terminal from which the bid has been uploaded will be taken into account.<sup>48</sup>

items.value: 8.3. The details of commission and processing fees payable to each intermediary and the timelines for payment shall be disclosed in the offer document and this shall be implemented strictly.<sup>49</sup>
items.md: 8.3. The details of commission and processing fees payable to each intermediary and the timelines for payment shall be disclosed in the offer document and this shall be implemented strictly.<sup>49</sup>

items.value: 8.4. Merchant Bankers shall ensure that an appropriate disclosure is made in the offer document specifying the range of fee that SCSBs would be entitled to for processing such ASBA forms procured by Intermediaries and submitted to SCSBs.<sup>50</sup>
items.md: 8.4. Merchant Bankers shall ensure that an appropriate disclosure is made in the offer document specifying the range of fee that SCSBs would be entitled to for processing such ASBA forms procured by Intermediaries and submitted to SCSBs.<sup>50</sup>

items.value: 8.5. Payment of Processing Fee to SCSBs<sup>51</sup>: To claim the processing fee, SCSBs shall make an application to the Merchant Bankers in the format prescribed in <u>Annexure XIII</u> with a copy to the Registrar to the Issue, subject to the following conditions:
items.md: 8.5. **Payment of Processing Fee to SCSBs<sup>51</sup>**: To claim the processing fee, SCSBs shall make an application to the Merchant Bankers in the format prescribed in <u>Annexure XIII</u> with a copy to the Registrar to the Issue, subject to the following conditions:

items.value: 8.5.1. The application shall be made no later than 30 days from the finalization of basis of allotment by Registrar to the Issue.
items.md: 8.5.1. The application shall be made no later than 30 days from the finalization of basis of allotment by Registrar to the Issue.

items.value: 8.5.2. The SCSBs shall make the application only after (i) unblocking of application amounts for each application received by such SCSB has been fully
items.md: 8.5.2. The SCSBs shall make the application only after (i) unblocking of application amounts for each application received by such SCSB has been fully

items.value: <u>Chapter 10: Use of Unified Payment Interface (UPI) in Public Issue</u><sup>54</sup>
items.md: # <u>Chapter 10: Use of Unified Payment Interface (UPI) in Public Issue</u><sup>54</sup>

items.value: SEBI has introduced the use of Unified Payments Interface (UPI) as a payment mechanism with Application Supported by Block Amount (ASBA) for applications in public issues by retail individual investors through intermediaries (Syndicate members, Registered Stock Brokers, Registrar and Transfer agent and Depository Participants). This payment mechanism would increase efficiency, eliminate the need for manual intervention at various stages.
items.md: SEBI has introduced the use of Unified Payments Interface (UPI) as a payment mechanism with Application Supported by Block Amount (ASBA) for applications in public issues by retail individual investors through intermediaries (Syndicate members, Registered Stock Brokers, Registrar and Transfer agent and Depository Participants). This payment mechanism would increase efficiency, eliminate the need for manual intervention at various stages.

items.value: New entities / mechanisms part of the public issue process using UPI with ASBA
items.md: **New entities / mechanisms part of the public issue process using UPI with ASBA**

1. SEBI has introduced the use of Unified Payments Interface (UPI) as a payment mechanism with Application Supported by Block Amount (ASBA) for applications in public issues by retail individual investors through intermediaries (Syndicate members, Registered Stock Brokers, Registrar and Transfer agent and Depository Participants). This payment mechanism would increase efficiency, eliminate the need for manual intervention at various stages.

2. **New entities / mechanisms part of the public issue process using UPI with ASBA**

items.value: 2.1. National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI): NPCI, a Reserve Bank of India (RBI) initiative, is an umbrella organization for all retail payments in India. It has been set up with the guidance and support of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and Indian Banks Association (IBA).
items.md: 2.1. **National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI):** NPCI, a Reserve Bank of India (RBI) initiative, is an umbrella organization for all retail payments in India. It has been set up with the guidance and support of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and Indian Banks Association (IBA).

items.value: 2.2. Unified Payments Interface (UPI): UPI is an instant payment system developed by the NPCI. It enables merging several banking features, seamless fund routing & merchant payments into one hood. UPI allows instant transfer of money between any two persons’ bank accounts using a payment address which uniquely identifies a person's bank a/c.
items.md: 2.2. **Unified Payments Interface (UPI):** UPI is an instant payment system developed by the NPCI. It enables merging several banking features, seamless fund routing & merchant payments into one hood. UPI allows instant transfer of money between any two persons’ bank accounts using a payment address which uniquely identifies a person's bank a/c.

items.value: 2.3. Sponsor Bank: Sponsor Bank means a Banker to the Issue registered with SEBI which is appointed by the Issuer to act as a conduit between the Stock Exchanges and NPCI in order to push the mandate collect requests and / or payment instructions of the retail investors into the UPI.
items.md: 2.3. **Sponsor Bank:** Sponsor Bank means a Banker to the Issue registered with SEBI which is appointed by the Issuer to act as a conduit between the Stock Exchanges and NPCI in order to push the mandate collect requests and / or payment instructions of the retail investors into the UPI.

items.value: 8.1. In order to ensure parity across the various channels for submitted applications, an investor making application using any of the aforesaid channel, shall use only his / her own bank account or only his/her own bank account linked UPI ID to make an application in public issues.

items.value: 8.2. Applications made using third party bank account or using third party linked bank account UPI ID are liable for rejection.

items.value: 9. Obligations of the Issuer
items.md: # 9. Obligations of the Issuer

items.value: 9.1. The issuer shall appoint one of the SCSBs as Sponsor Bank to act as a conduit between the Stock Exchanges and NPCI in order to push the mandate collect requests and / or payment instructions of the retail investors into the UPI;

items.value: 9.2. The Sponsor Bank appointed by the issuer may be the same as the bank with whom the public issue account has been opened.
