items.value: 12. Retention of forms by Intermediaries<sup>57</sup>
items.md: # 12. Retention of forms by Intermediaries<sup>57</sup>

items.value: 12.1. Intermediaries shall retain physical application forms submitted by retail individual investors with UPI as a payment mechanism, for a period of six months and thereafter forward the same to the issuer/Registrar to Issue. However, in case of Electronic forms, "printouts" of such applications need not be retained or sent to the issuer. Intermediaries shall, at all times, maintain the electronic records relating to such forms for a minimum period of three years.
items.md: 12.1. Intermediaries shall retain physical application forms submitted by retail individual investors with UPI as a payment mechanism, for a period of six months and thereafter forward the same to the issuer/Registrar to Issue. However, in case of Electronic forms, "printouts" of such applications need not be retained or sent to the issuer. Intermediaries shall, at all times, maintain the electronic records relating to such forms for a minimum period of three years.

items.value: <u>Streamlining the IPO Process</u>

items.value: 13.3. In terms of Regulations 23(2), 23(4), 23(5), 271, Schedules I & II of the ICDR Regulations 2018, the Lead Manager is the nodal entity for any issues arising out of a public issuance process and the timelines, processes and compensation policy defined in this circular shall form part of the agreement(s) that shall be signed among the intermediaries. Lead Managers shall ensure the adherence of timelines, processes and compensation policy by intermediaries.
items.md: 13.3. In terms of Regulations 23(2), 23(4), 23(5), 271, Schedules I & II of the ICDR Regulations 2018, the Lead Manager is the nodal entity for any issues arising out of a public issuance process and the timelines, processes and compensation policy defined in this circular shall form part of the agreement(s) that shall be signed among the intermediaries. Lead Managers shall ensure the adherence of timelines, processes and compensation policy by intermediaries.

items.value: 13.4. In order to ensure timely response with regard to IPO process, SCSBs shall identify the nodal officer for IPO applications processed through UPI as a payment mechanism and submit the details as per <u>Annexure XVII</u> to SEBI. For ease of reference, the details of nodal officers of SCSBs shall be hosted on the SEBI Website.
items.md: 13.4. In order to ensure timely response with regard to IPO process, SCSBs shall identify the nodal officer for IPO applications processed through UPI as a payment mechanism and submit the details as per <u>Annexure XVII</u> to SEBI. For ease of reference, the details of nodal officers of SCSBs shall be hosted on the SEBI Website.

items.value: 13.5. For ensuring timely information to investors, SCSBs/UPI Apps eligible for Public Issues shall send SMS alerts to Investors for all ASBA applications and may also provide the Invoice in the Inbox as an additional feature to verify the UPI mandate details. The SMS/Invoice in the Inbox shall include the details as prescribed in <u>Annexure XVIII</u>.
items.md: 13.5. For ensuring timely information to investors, SCSBs/UPI Apps eligible for Public Issues shall send SMS alerts to Investors for all ASBA applications and may also provide the *Invoice in the Inbox* as an additional feature to verify the UPI mandate details. The *SMS/Invoice in the Inbox* shall include the details as prescribed in <u>Annexure XVIII</u>.

items.value: 13.6. For ease of doing business, Sponsor Banks shall host a web portal for intermediaries (closed user group) from the date of IPO opening till the date of listing with details of statistics of mandate blocks/unblocks, performance of Apps and UPI Handles, down-time/network latency (if any) across intermediaries and any such processes having an impact/bearing on the IPO bidding process.

items.value: 13.9. <u>Cancelled/Withdrawn/Deleted applications</u><sup>61</sup>: Registrars to an Issue (RTI) shall submit the details of cancelled/withdrawn/deleted applications to SCSB’s on daily basis within 60 minutes of bid closure time from the Issue opening date till Issue closing date (T) by obtaining the same from Stock Exchanges. SCSB’s shall unblock such applications by the closing hours of the bank day and submit the confirmation to Lead Managers and RTA on daily basis, as per the format prescribed in <u>Annexure XIX</u>.
items.md: 13.9. <u>**Cancelled/Withdrawn/Deleted applications**</u><sup>61</sup>: Registrars to an Issue (RTI) shall submit the details of cancelled/withdrawn/deleted applications to SCSB’s on daily basis within 60 minutes of bid closure time from the Issue opening date till Issue closing date (T) by obtaining the same from Stock Exchanges. SCSB’s shall unblock such applications by the closing hours of the bank day and submit the confirmation to Lead Managers and RTA on daily basis, as per the format prescribed in <u>Annexure XIX</u>.

items.value: 13.10. <u>Unblocking of UPI Mandates</u><sup>62</sup>: SEBI had endeavored that the unblock of applications shall be completed on T+2 (T: Issue Closing Date). However, in view of the complaints received pertaining to delayed unblock, the following process shall be implemented by intermediaries.
items.md: 13.10. <u>**Unblocking of UPI Mandates**</u><sup>62</sup>: SEBI had endeavored that the unblock of applications shall be completed on T+2 (T: Issue Closing Date). However, in view of the complaints received pertaining to delayed unblock, the following process shall be implemented by intermediaries.

items.value: 13.10.1. Sponsor Banks shall execute the online mandate revoke file for Non-Allottees/Partial Allottees on T+2. Subsequently, any pending applications for unblock shall be submitted to RTA, not later than T+2.
items.md: 13.10.1. Sponsor Banks shall execute the online mandate revoke file for Non-Allottees/Partial Allottees on T+2. Subsequently, any pending applications for unblock shall be submitted to RTA, not later than T+2.

items.value: 13.10.2. Subsequently, RTI shall submit the bank-wise pending UPI applications for unblock to SCSB’s along with the allotment file, not later than T+2. The allotment file shall include all applications pertaining to full-allotment / partial-allotment / non-allotment / cancelled / withdrawn / deleted applications etc.
items.md: 13.10.2. Subsequently, RTI shall submit the bank-wise pending UPI applications for unblock to SCSB’s along with the allotment file, not later than T+2. The allotment file shall include all applications pertaining to full-allotment / partial-allotment / non-allotment / cancelled / withdrawn / deleted applications etc.

items.value: 13.10.3. SCSB’s shall ensure that the unblock for non-allotted/partial-allotted applications is completed by the closing hours of bank day on T+2. SCSB’s shall submit the confirmation on the same, to Lead Managers and RTA, not later than T+2. Such report shall be submitted as per the format prescribed in <u>Annexure XX</u><sup>63</sup>.
items.md: 13.10.3. SCSB’s shall ensure that the unblock for non-allotted/partial-allotted applications is completed by the closing hours of bank day on T+2. SCSB’s shall submit the confirmation on the same, to Lead Managers and RTA, not later than T+2. Such report shall be submitted as per the format prescribed in <u>Annexure XX</u><sup>63</sup>.

items.value: 13.11. SCSBs failing to provide the details as per Annexure XX to Lead Managers/RTI, not later than T+2 shall be liable to face appropriate action under Securities Laws. In order to provide an efficient redressal mechanism for complaints from investors pertaining to Block/Unblock of funds, to protect the interests of investors and to avoid any opportunity loss, the compensation structure as prescribed in <u>Annexure XXI</u> shall be applicable. SCSBs shall compensate all ASBA applications processed by the SCSBs, immediately on the date of receipt of complaint from the investor.
items.md: 13.11. SCSBs failing to provide the details as per Annexure XX to Lead Managers/RTI, not later than T+2 shall be liable to face appropriate action under Securities Laws. In order to provide an efficient redressal mechanism for complaints from investors pertaining to Block/Unblock of funds, to protect the interests of investors and to avoid any opportunity loss, the compensation structure as prescribed in <u>Annexure XXI</u> shall be applicable. SCSBs shall compensate all ASBA applications processed by the SCSBs, immediately on the date of receipt of complaint from the investor.

items.value: Chapter 11: Timeline for listing of shares in Public Issue<sup>66</sup>
items.md: # Chapter 11: Timeline for listing of shares in Public Issue<sup>66</sup>

items.value: Consequent to extensive consultation with the market participants and considering the public comments received pursuant to consultation paper on the aforesaid subject matter, it was decided to reduce the time taken for listing of specified securities after the closure of public issue to 3 working days (T+3 days) as against the present requirement of 6 working days (T+6 days); ‘T’ being issue closing date.
items.md: Consequent to extensive consultation with the market participants and considering the public comments received pursuant to consultation paper on the aforesaid subject matter, it was decided to reduce the time taken for listing of specified securities after the closure of public issue to 3 working days (T+3 days) as against the present requirement of 6 working days (T+6 days); ‘T’ being issue closing date.

items.value: Accordingly, the revised timelines for listing of specified securities and various activities involved in the public issue process are specified in <u>Annexure XXIII</u>.
items.md: Accordingly, the revised timelines for listing of specified securities and various activities involved in the public issue process are specified in <u>Annexure XXIII</u>.

items.value: The T+3 timeline for listing shall be appropriately disclosed in the Offer Documents of public issues.
items.md: The T+3 timeline for listing shall be appropriately disclosed in the Offer Documents of public issues.

items.value: The timelines for submission of application, allotment of securities, unblocking of application monies and listing shall prominently be made a part of pre-issue, issue opening and issue closing advertisements issued by the Issuer for public issues in terms of the ICDR Regulations 2018.
items.md: The timelines for submission of application, allotment of securities, unblocking of application monies and listing shall prominently be made a part of pre-issue, issue opening and issue closing advertisements issued by the Issuer for public issues in terms of the ICDR Regulations 2018.

1. Consequent to extensive consultation with the market participants and considering the public comments received pursuant to consultation paper on the aforesaid subject matter, it was decided to reduce the time taken for listing of specified securities after the closure of public issue to 3 working days (T+3 days) as against the present requirement of 6 working days (T+6 days); ‘T’ being issue closing date.

2. Accordingly, the revised timelines for listing of specified securities and various activities involved in the public issue process are specified in <u>Annexure XXIII</u>.

3. The T+3 timeline for listing shall be appropriately disclosed in the Offer Documents of public issues.

4. The timelines for submission of application, allotment of securities, unblocking of application monies and listing shall prominently be made a part of pre-issue, issue opening and issue closing advertisements issued by the Issuer for public issues in terms of the ICDR Regulations 2018.

items.value: General Instructions:
items.md: **General Instructions:**

items.value: For Direct Bank ASBA and Syndicate ASBA applications, prior to blocking of ASBA application monies in the bank account of the applicant, the SCSBs shall ensure the following:
items.md: For Direct Bank ASBA and Syndicate ASBA applications, prior to blocking of ASBA application monies in the bank account of the applicant, the SCSBs shall ensure the following:
5. For Direct Bank ASBA and Syndicate ASBA applications, prior to blocking of ASBA application monies in the bank account of the applicant, the SCSBs shall ensure the following:

items.value: 5.1. The PAN mentioned in the application matches with the PAN linked to the bank account of the applicant maintained with the bank.
items.md: 5.1. The PAN mentioned in the application matches with the PAN linked to the bank account of the applicant maintained with the bank.

items.value: A confirmation on the above shall be submitted by SCSBs to the Registrar to an Issue along with the Final Certificate.
items.md: *A confirmation on the above shall be submitted by SCSBs to the Registrar to an Issue along with the Final Certificate.*

items.value: 5.2. The PAN linked to the bank account of the applicant shall be part of the bidding data on the Stock Exchanges platform.
items.md: 5.2. The PAN linked to the bank account of the applicant shall be part of the bidding data on the Stock Exchanges platform.

items.value: The Registrar to an Issue shall undertake third-party verification of the applications by matching the PAN available in the demat account with the PAN available in the bank account of the applicant. In instances of mismatch, such applications shall continue to be considered as invalid applications for finalising the basis of allotment.
items.md: The Registrar to an Issue shall undertake third-party verification of the applications by matching the PAN available in the demat account with the PAN available in the bank account of the applicant. In instances of mismatch, such applications shall continue to be considered as invalid applications for finalising the basis of allotment.

items.value: Lock-in of pre-issue shares has been made effective in compliance with the ICDR Regulations 2018. Further, the operationalization of lock-in is in line with the SOP of Depositories issued vide Circular/Communique dated August 8, 2023.
items.md: Lock-in of pre-issue shares has been made effective in compliance with the ICDR Regulations 2018. Further, the operationalization of lock-in is in line with the SOP of Depositories issued vide Circular/Communique dated August 8, 2023.

items.value: The compensation to investors for delay in unblocking of ASBA application monies (if any) shall be computed from T+3 day.
items.md: The compensation to investors for delay in unblocking of ASBA application monies (if any) shall be computed from T+3 day.

6. The Registrar to an Issue shall undertake third-party verification of the applications by matching the PAN available in the demat account with the PAN available in the bank account of the applicant. In instances of mismatch, such applications shall continue to be considered as invalid applications for finalising the basis of allotment.

7. Lock-in of pre-issue shares has been made effective in compliance with the ICDR Regulations 2018. Further, the operationalization of lock-in is in line with the SOP of Depositories issued vide Circular/Communique dated August 8, 2023.

8. The compensation to investors for delay in unblocking of ASBA application monies (if any) shall be computed from T+3 day.

items.md: # <u>Chapter 12: Withdrawal of Master Circular on issuance of No Objection Certificate (NOC) for release of 1% of Issue Amount<sup>70</sup></u>

items.value: In order to facilitate ease of doing business to Issuer company, the requirement to deposit 1% of the issue size available for subscription to the public with the designated stock exchange by the Issuer company under regulation 38 (1) of SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018 (ICDR Regulations) has been dispensed with.
items.md: In order to facilitate ease of doing business to Issuer company, the requirement to deposit 1% of the issue size available for subscription to the public with the designated stock exchange by the Issuer company under regulation 38 (1) of SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018 (ICDR Regulations) has been dispensed with.

items.value: <u>Chapter 13: Industry Standards on Key Performance Indicators (“KPIs”) Disclosures in the draft Offer Document and Offer Document<sup>71</sup></u>
items.md: # <u>Chapter 13: Industry Standards on Key Performance Indicators (“KPIs”) Disclosures in the draft Offer Document and Offer Document<sup>71</sup></u>

items.value: In order to facilitate uniform approach in identification and disclosure practices of Key Performance Indicators (“KPIs”), the Industry Standards Forum (“ISF”) comprising of representatives from three industry associations, viz. ASSOCHAM, CII and FICCI, under the aegis of the Stock Exchanges, has formulated industry standards, in consultation with SEBI, for effective implementation of the requirement to disclose KPIs in the draft offer document and offer document as per the provisions of ICDR Regulations. The industry associations which are part of ISF (ASSOCHAM, FICCI, and CII) and the stock exchanges shall publish the industry standards on their websites.
items.md: In order to facilitate uniform approach in identification and disclosure practices of Key Performance Indicators (“KPIs”), the Industry Standards Forum (“ISF”) comprising of representatives from three industry associations, viz. [ASSOCHAM,](https://www.assocham.org/) CII and [FICCI,](https://ficci.in/api/home) under the aegis of the Stock Exchanges, has formulated industry standards, in consultation with SEBI, for effective implementation of the requirement to disclose KPIs in the draft offer document and offer document as per the provisions of ICDR Regulations. The industry associations which are part of ISF (**ASSOCHAM, FICCI, and CII**) and the stock exchanges shall publish the industry standards on their websites.

items.value: The Issuer Companies and Merchant Bankers shall follow the aforesaid industry standards to ensure compliance with the requirement to disclose KPIs in the draft offer document and offer document as per the provisions of ICDR Regulations.
items.md: The Issuer Companies and Merchant Bankers shall follow the aforesaid industry standards to ensure compliance with the requirement to disclose KPIs in the draft offer document and offer document as per the provisions of ICDR Regulations.

items.value: This chapter is applicable for all draft offer documents / offer documents filed with SEBI / Stock Exchanges on or after April 1, 2025.
items.md: This chapter is applicable for all draft offer documents / offer documents filed with SEBI / Stock Exchanges on or after April 1, 2025.

1. In order to facilitate uniform approach in identification and disclosure practices of Key Performance Indicators (“KPIs”), the Industry Standards Forum (“ISF”) comprising of representatives from three industry associations, viz. [ASSOCHAM,](https://www.assocham.org/) CII and [FICCI,](https://ficci.in/api/home) under the aegis of the Stock Exchanges, has formulated industry standards, in consultation with SEBI, for effective implementation of the requirement to disclose KPIs in the draft offer document and offer document as per the provisions of ICDR Regulations. The industry associations which are part of ISF (**ASSOCHAM, FICCI, and CII**) and the stock exchanges shall publish the industry standards on their websites.

2. The Issuer Companies and Merchant Bankers shall follow the aforesaid industry standards to ensure compliance with the requirement to disclose KPIs in the draft offer document and offer document as per the provisions of ICDR Regulations.

3. This chapter is applicable for all draft offer documents / offer documents filed with SEBI / Stock Exchanges on or after April 1, 2025.

items.value: Chapter 14: Enabling T+2 trading of Bonus shares where T is the record date <sup>72</sup>
items.md: # Chapter 14: Enabling T+2 trading of Bonus shares where T is the record date <sup>72</sup>

items.value: As a part of the continuing endeavor to streamline the process of Bonus issue of equity shares, in consultation with the market participants, it has been decided to reduce the time taken for credit of bonus shares and trading of such shares, from the record date of the Bonus Issue under SEBI (ICDR) Regulations, 2018.
items.md: As a part of the continuing endeavor to streamline the process of Bonus issue of equity shares, in consultation with the market participants, it has been decided to reduce the time taken for credit of bonus shares and trading of such shares, from the record date of the Bonus Issue under SEBI (ICDR) Regulations, 2018.

items.value: The operational procedure to implement the above is as given below:
items.md: The operational procedure to implement the above is as given below:

1. As a part of the continuing endeavor to streamline the process of Bonus issue of equity shares, in consultation with the market participants, it has been decided to reduce the time taken for credit of bonus shares and trading of such shares, from the record date of the Bonus Issue under SEBI (ICDR) Regulations, 2018.

2. The operational procedure to implement the above is as given below:

items.value: 2.1. The Issuer proposing a bonus issue shall apply for the in-principle approval under Regulation 28(1) of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, to the Stock Exchange within 5 working days from the date of board meeting approving the Bonus issue.
items.md: 2.1. The Issuer proposing a bonus issue shall apply for the in-principle approval under Regulation 28(1) of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, to the Stock Exchange within 5 working days from the date of board meeting approving the Bonus issue.

items.value: 2.2. The Issuer while fixing and intimating the record date (T day) to the Stock Exchange as required under Regulation 42(1) of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, for the proposed bonus issue, shall also take on record deemed date of allotment on next working date of record date (T+1 day).
items.md: 2.2. The Issuer while fixing and intimating the record date (T day) to the Stock Exchange as required under Regulation 42(1) of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, for the proposed bonus issue, shall also take on record deemed date of allotment on next working date of record date (T+1 day).

items.value: 2.3. Upon receipt of intimation of the record date (T Day) and requisite documents from the Issuer, the Stock Exchange(s) shall issue notification accepting the record date and notifying the number of shares considered in the bonus issue. The notification shall include the deemed date of allotment (T+1 day).
items.md: 2.3. Upon receipt of intimation of the record date (T Day) and requisite documents from the Issuer, the Stock Exchange(s) shall issue notification accepting the record date and notifying the number of shares considered in the bonus issue. The notification shall include the deemed date of allotment (T+1 day).

items.value: 2.4. After issuance of notification issued by the Stock Exchange for acceptance of record date, the Issuers shall ensure submission of the requisite documents to Depositories for credit of bonus shares in the depository system latest by 12 P.M. of next working day of the record date (i.e. T+1 day).
items.md: 2.4. After issuance of notification issued by the Stock Exchange for acceptance of record date, the Issuers shall ensure submission of the requisite documents to Depositories for credit of bonus shares in the depository system latest by 12 P.M. of next working day of the record date (i.e. T+1 day).

items.value: 2.5. The Issuer shall ensure upload of the distinctive number (DN) ranges in the DN database of the depository and stock exchange(s) shall ensure updation of relevant dates before credit of bonus shares.
items.md: 2.5. The Issuer shall ensure upload of the distinctive number (DN) ranges in the DN database of the depository and stock exchange(s) shall ensure updation of relevant dates before credit of bonus shares.

items.value: 2.6. The shares allotted pursuant to the bonus issue shall be made available for trading on the next working date of allotment (T+2 day).
items.md: 2.6. The shares allotted pursuant to the bonus issue shall be made available for trading on the next working date of allotment (T+2 day).

items.value: 2.7. The directions issued pursuant to SEBI Circular No. CIR/MRD/DP/21/2012 dated August 02, 2012 and CIR/MRD/DP/ 24 /2012 dated September 11, 2012 requiring credit of bonus shares in temporary ISIN shall be exempted in case of bonus issue of equity shares, and credit of shares directly in permanent ISIN (existing ISIN) shall be permitted in case of bonus issue of equity shares.
items.md: 2.7. The directions issued pursuant to SEBI Circular No. CIR/MRD/DP/21/2012 dated August 02, 2012 and CIR/MRD/DP/ 24 /2012 dated September 11, 2012 requiring credit of bonus shares in temporary ISIN shall be exempted in case of bonus issue of equity shares, and credit of shares directly in permanent ISIN (existing ISIN) shall be permitted in case of bonus issue of equity shares.

items.value: The Exchange(s) and Depositories are advised to make amendments to the relevant bye-laws, rules and regulations for the implementation of the above decision, as may be applicable.
items.md: The Exchange(s) and Depositories are advised to make amendments to the relevant bye-laws, rules and regulations for the implementation of the above decision, as may be applicable.
3. The Exchange(s) and Depositories are advised to make amendments to the relevant bye-laws, rules and regulations for the implementation of the above decision, as may be applicable.

items.value: <u>Chapter 15: Redemption of Indian Depository Receipts (IDRs) into Underlying Equity Shares</u> <sup>73</sup>
items.md: # <u>Chapter 15: Redemption of Indian Depository Receipts (IDRs) into Underlying Equity Shares</u> <sup>73</sup>

items.value: In order to facilitate foreign issuers to raise funds from the Indian capital markets through IDRs and enable investors in the domestic market to have investment opportunities in the securities of major multi-national companies listed on well developed markets, a legal framework was created by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA), Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and SEBI.
items.md: In order to facilitate foreign issuers to raise funds from the Indian capital markets through IDRs and enable investors in the domestic market to have investment opportunities in the securities of major multi-national companies listed on well developed markets, a legal framework was created by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA), Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and SEBI.

items.value: In order to encourage more number of foreign companies to issue IDRs in the Indian market and also to enable the investors to take informed investment decision, it has been decided to provide a detailed roadmap and guidelines for the future IDR issuances as well as for the existing listed IDRs.
items.md: In order to encourage more number of foreign companies to issue IDRs in the Indian market and also to enable the investors to take informed investment decision, it has been decided to provide a detailed roadmap and guidelines for the future IDR issuances as well as for the existing listed IDRs.

items.value: All the IDRs shall have partial two-way fungibility. The partial two-way fungibility means that the IDRs can be converted into underlying equity shares and the underlying equity shares can be converted into IDRs within the available headroom. The headroom for this purpose shall be the number of IDRs originally issued minus the number of IDRs outstanding, which is further adjusted for IDRs redeemed into underlying equity shares ("Headroom").
items.md: All the IDRs shall have partial two-way fungibility. The partial two-way fungibility means that the IDRs can be converted into underlying equity shares and the underlying equity shares can be converted into IDRs within the available headroom. The headroom for this purpose shall be the number of IDRs originally issued minus the number of IDRs outstanding, which is further adjusted for IDRs redeemed into underlying equity shares ("Headroom").

items.value: The broad guidelines for fungibility of IDR issuances are given below:
items.md: The broad guidelines for fungibility of IDR issuances are given below:

1. In order to facilitate foreign issuers to raise funds from the Indian capital markets through IDRs and enable investors in the domestic market to have investment opportunities in the securities of major multi-national companies listed on well developed markets, a legal framework was created by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA), Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and SEBI.

2. In order to encourage more number of foreign companies to issue IDRs in the Indian market and also to enable the investors to take informed investment decision, it has been decided to provide a detailed roadmap and guidelines for the future IDR issuances as well as for the existing listed IDRs.

3. All the IDRs shall have partial two-way fungibility. The partial two-way fungibility means that the IDRs can be converted into underlying equity shares and the underlying equity shares can be converted into IDRs within the available headroom. The headroom for this purpose shall be the number of IDRs originally issued minus the number of IDRs outstanding, which is further adjusted for IDRs redeemed into underlying equity shares ("Headroom").

4. The broad guidelines for fungibility of IDR issuances are given below:

items.value: 4.1. IDRs shall not be redeemable into underlying equity shares before the expiry of one-year period from the date of listing of IDRs.
items.md: 4.1. IDRs shall not be redeemable into underlying equity shares before the expiry of one-year period from the date of listing of IDRs.

items.value: 4.2. After completion of one year period from the date of listing of IDRs, the issuer shall, provide two-way fungibility of IDRs.
items.md: 4.2. After completion of one year period from the date of listing of IDRs, the issuer shall, provide two-way fungibility of IDRs.

items.value: 4.3. IDR fungibility shall be provided on a continuous basis.
items.md: 4.3. IDR fungibility shall be provided on a continuous basis.

items.value: 4.4. The issuer shall provide said fungibility to IDR holders in any of the following ways:
items.md: 4.4. The issuer shall provide said fungibility to IDR holders in any of the following ways:

items.value: 4.4.1. converting IDRs into underlying shares; or
items.md: 4.4.1. converting IDRs into underlying shares; or

items.value: 4.4.2. converting IDRs into underlying shares and selling the underlying shares in the foreign market where the shares of the issuer are listed and providing the sale proceeds to the IDR holders; or
items.md: 4.4.2. converting IDRs into underlying shares and selling the underlying shares in the foreign market where the shares of the issuer are listed and providing the sale proceeds to the IDR holders; or

items.value: 4.4.3. both the above options may be provided to IDR holders
items.md: 4.4.3. both the above options may be provided to IDR holders

items.value: Provided that the option once exercised and disclosed by the issuer at the time of offering the IDRs to public cannot be changed without the specific approval of SEBI
items.md: Provided that the option once exercised and disclosed by the issuer at the time of offering the IDRs to public cannot be changed without the specific approval of SEBI

items.value: 4.5. All the IDRs that have been applied for fungibility by the holder shall be transferred to IDR redemption account at the time of application. The issuer shall take necessary
items.md: 4.5. All the IDRs that have been applied for fungibility by the holder shall be transferred to IDR redemption account at the time of application. The issuer shall take necessary

items.value: 4.6. The Issuer may receive requests from the holders of underlying shares and convert these into IDRs subject to the Headroom available with respect to the number of IDRs originally issued subject to the guidelines prescribed by SEBI & Reserve Bank of India (“RBI”) from time to time.
items.md: 4.6. The Issuer may receive requests from the holders of underlying shares and convert these into IDRs subject to the Headroom available with respect to the number of IDRs originally issued subject to the guidelines prescribed by SEBI & Reserve Bank of India (“RBI”) from time to time.

items.value: 4.7. In case of option of converting the IDRs into underlying equity shares and providing the sale proceeds to the IDR holders is exercised, the issuer shall disclose the range of fixed/variable costs in percentage terms upfront and ensure that all the costs together shall not exceed 5% of the sale proceeds.
items.md: 4.7. In case of option of converting the IDRs into underlying equity shares and providing the sale proceeds to the IDR holders is exercised, the issuer shall disclose the range of fixed/variable costs in percentage terms upfront and ensure that all the costs together shall not exceed 5% of the sale proceeds.

items.value: 4.8. The issuer on a continuous basis shall disclose available Headroom and significant conversion / reconversion transactions.
items.md: 4.8. The issuer on a continuous basis shall disclose available Headroom and significant conversion / reconversion transactions.

items.value: 4.9. The issuer shall lay down the detailed procedures while taking into consideration the above broad guidelines in addition to other norms specified by SEBI and RBI, from time to time.
items.md: 4.9. The issuer shall lay down the detailed procedures while taking into consideration the above broad guidelines in addition to other norms specified by SEBI and RBI, from time to time.

items.value: <u>Chapter 16: Adjustment of differential pricing amount at the time of application for allotment of specified securities<sup>74</sup></u>
items.md: # <u>Chapter 16: Adjustment of differential pricing amount at the time of application for allotment of specified securities<sup>74</sup></u>

items.value: This refers to the provisions of Regulation 30, 128, 190 and 251 of ICDR Regulations, which provide for offer of specified securities at different prices to certain investors.
items.md: This refers to the provisions of Regulation 30, 128, 190 and 251 of ICDR Regulations, which provide for offer of specified securities at different prices to certain investors.

items.value: It is clarified that the investors eligible for differential pricing in public issues to make payment at a price net of discount, any, at the time of bidding itself, and in this context, it is clarified that -
items.md: It is clarified that the investors eligible for differential pricing in public issues to make payment at a price net of discount, any, at the time of bidding itself, and in this context, it is clarified that -

1. This refers to the provisions of Regulation 30, 128, 190 and 251 of ICDR Regulations, which provide for offer of specified securities at different prices to certain investors.

2. It is clarified that the investors eligible for differential pricing in public issues to make payment at a price net of discount, any, at the time of bidding itself, and in this context, it is clarified that -

items.value: 2.1. Merchant Bankers shall ensure that appropriate disclosures are given in the offer document / application forms to the effect that investors eligible for discount can make payment after adjusting the discount, if any. It shall be disclosed that such investors shall in the relevant column indicate the bid price before adjusting for discount, if any. Further, it shall be clearly disclosed under what circumstances application would be liable for rejection in case of errors, if any, in this regard.
items.md: 2.1. Merchant Bankers shall ensure that appropriate disclosures are given in the offer document / application forms to the effect that investors eligible for discount can make payment after adjusting the discount, if any. It shall be disclosed that such investors shall in the relevant column indicate the bid price before adjusting for discount, if any. Further, it shall be clearly disclosed under what circumstances application would be liable for rejection in case of errors, if any, in this regard.

items.value: 2.2. Stock Exchanges shall ensure that appropriate provisions for discount adjustment are provided in the bidding platform. Whenever the net payment (post discount) is more than two lakh rupees, the bidding system should be capable of ensuring that such applications are not eligible for discount.
items.md: 2.2. Stock Exchanges shall ensure that appropriate provisions for discount adjustment are provided in the bidding platform. Whenever the net payment (post discount) is more than two lakh rupees, the bidding system should be capable of ensuring that such applications are not eligible for discount.

items.value: 2.3. Syndicate /Sub syndicate members and SCSBs shall enter the bid price as indicated by the applicant in the price column.
items.md: 2.3. Syndicate /Sub syndicate members and SCSBs shall enter the bid price as indicated by the applicant in the price column.

items.value: 2.4. Segregation of Investor Categories (i.e. two lakh rupees for retail category), shall be based on the net payment amount (after adjusting for discount).
items.md: 2.4. Segregation of Investor Categories (i.e. two lakh rupees for retail category), shall be based on the net payment amount (after adjusting for discount).

items.value: 2.5. SCSBs shall carry out the necessary system changes at their end, if any.
items.md: 2.5. SCSBs shall carry out the necessary system changes at their end, if any.

items.value: Merchant Bankers shall create awareness among the investors eligible for discount on account of differential pricing to make payment at price net of discount.
items.md: Merchant Bankers shall create awareness among the investors eligible for discount on account of differential pricing to make payment at price net of discount.
3. Merchant Bankers shall create awareness among the investors eligible for discount on account of differential pricing to make payment at price net of discount.

Filing of Letter of Offer with Stock Exchanges and SEBI
Receipt of BENPOS on Record date (at the end of the day)
Dispatch / Communication to the shareholders of Letter of Offer
Publication of advertisement for completion of dispatch
Publication of advertisement for disclosing details of specific investor(s)
Issue opening and commencement of trading in REs (Issue to be kept open for minimum 7 days as per Companies Act, 2013)
Closure of REs trading (3 working days prior to issue closure date)
Closure of off-market transfer of REs

*If the Issuer is making a Rights Issue of convertible debt instruments, wherein shareholder’s approval is required, then the notice for 2nd Board meeting to fix record date, price, entitlement ratio etc. would be given on the date of receiving shareholders’ approval and the remaining timeline would be adjusted accordingly.
items.html:
TABLE A: Indicative timeline of broad activities involved in Rights Issue from the date of approval of Board of Directors of the Issuer till the date of closure of Rights Issue
Sr. No. Broad activities performed during Rights Issue Process Timelines (Working Days)
1. 1st Board meeting for approval of rights issue T (T being the date of Board of Directors of the Issuer approving the Rights Issue)
2. Notice for 2nd Board meeting to fix record date, price, entitlement ratio etc. Subject to Board’s/ shareholders’ approval* T*
3. Application by the issuer for seeking in-principle approval along with filing of Draft Letter of Offer with Stock Exchanges T+1
4. Receipt of in-principle approval from Stock Exchanges T+3
5. 2nd Board meeting for fixing record date, price, entitlement ratio etc. T+4
6. Filing of Letter of Offer with Stock Exchanges and SEBI T+5 to T+7
7. Record Date T+8
8. Receipt of BENPOS on Record date (at the end of the day) T+8
9. Credit of Right Entitlements (REs) T+9
10. Dispatch / Communication to the shareholders of Letter of Offer T+10
11. Publication of advertisement for completion of dispatch T+11
12. Publication of advertisement for disclosing details of specific investor(s) T+11
13. Issue opening and commencement of trading in REs (Issue to be kept open for minimum 7 days as per Companies Act, 2013) T+14
14. Validation of Bids T+14 to T+20
15. Closure of REs trading (3 working days prior to issue closure date) T+17
16. Closure of off-market transfer of REs T+19
17. Issue closure T+20
*If the Issuer is making a Rights Issue of convertible debt instruments, wherein shareholder’s approval is required, then the notice for 2nd Board meeting to fix record date, price, entitlement ratio etc. would be given on the date of receiving shareholders’ approval and the remaining timeline would be adjusted accordingly.

rows: Broad activities performed during Rights Issue Process
1<sup>st</sup> Board meeting for approval of rights issue
T (T being the date of Board of Directors of the Issuer approving the Rights Issue)
Notice for 2<sup>nd</sup> Board meeting to fix record date, price, entitlement ratio etc. Subject to Board’s/ shareholders’ approval*
Application by the issuer for seeking in-principle approval along with filing of Draft Letter of Offer with Stock Exchanges
Receipt of in-principle approval from Stock Exchanges
2<sup>nd</sup> Board meeting for fixing record date, price, entitlement ratio etc.
Filing of Letter of Offer with Stock Exchanges and SEBI
Receipt of BENPOS on Record date (at the end of the day)
Dispatch / Communication to the shareholders of Letter of Offer
Publication of advertisement for completion of dispatch
Publication of advertisement for disclosing details of specific investor(s)
Issue opening and commencement of trading in REs (Issue to be kept open for minimum 7 days as per Companies Act, 2013)
Closure of REs trading (3 working days prior to issue closure date)
Closure of off-market transfer of REs

*If the Issuer is making a Rights Issue of convertible debt instruments, wherein shareholder’s approval is required, then the notice for 2<sup>nd</sup> Board meeting to fix record date, price, entitlement ratio etc. would be given on the date of receiving shareholders’ approval and the remaining timeline would be adjusted accordingly.
T (T being the date of closure of issue)
Suspension of RE ISIN (immediately on issue closure)
RTA obtains bid file from Stock Exchanges (SEs)
Reverting for correction files to SCSBs for ASBA bids
Receiving rectified/final bid data from SEs
Receipt of final certificate from the SCSBs
Co-ordination with SCSBs for pending final certificates for ASBA application
Complete reconciliation of valid ASBA, REs holding and technical rejection.
Basis of allotment to be carried out by RTAs in coordination with Designated Stock Exchange (DSE)
Stock Exchange to approve the basis of allotment
Transfer of funds from ASBA accounts to allotment account for allottees and refund account and unblocking of accounts for non-allottees
Listing application to be made to SEs
Instructions to dispatch of allotment and refund/unblocking intimations
Receiving in-principle listing approval for corporate action
Submit application with depositories for credit to respective demat shareholder account
Receipt of credit confirmation from NSDL/CDSL
Filing Documents with SEs for trading approval
Publication of basis of allotment advertisement in newspaper and submission of same with SEs
Receipt of trading approval from SEs
Shifting of shares from temporary ISIN to live ISIN
Transfer of funds from Rights escrow account to Issuer’s monitoring account
Commencement of trading of shares issued pursuant to rights issue
Submission of media compliance report with SEI

17. Issue closure T+20
*If the Issuer is making a Rights Issue of convertible debt instruments, wherein shareholder’s approval is required, then the notice for 2<sup>nd</sup> Board meeting to fix record date, price, entitlement ratio etc. would be given on the date of receiving shareholders’ approval and the remaining timeline would be adjusted accordingly.
1. Issue closure T (T being the date of closure of issue)
2. Suspension of RE ISIN (immediately on issue closure) T
3. RTA obtains bid file from Stock Exchanges (SEs) T
4. Reverting for correction files to SCSBs for ASBA bids T
5. Receiving rectified/final bid data from SEs T
6. Receipt of final certificate from the SCSBs T
7. Co-ordination with SCSBs for pending final certificates for ASBA application T
8. Complete reconciliation of valid ASBA, REs holding and technical rejection. T
9. Basis of allotment to be carried out by RTAs in coordination with Designated Stock Exchange (DSE) T+1
10. Stock Exchange to approve the basis of allotment T+1
11. Transfer of funds from ASBA accounts to allotment account for allottees and refund account and unblocking of accounts for non-allottees T+1
12. Listing application to be made to SEs T+1
13. Instructions to dispatch of allotment and refund/unblocking intimations T+2
14. Receiving in-principle listing approval for corporate action T+2
15. Submit application with depositories for credit to respective demat shareholder account T+2
16. Receipt of credit confirmation from NSDL/CDSL T+2
17. Filing Documents with SEs for trading approval T+2
18. Publication of basis of allotment advertisement in newspaper and submission of same with SEs T+2
19. Receipt of trading approval from SEs T+2
20. Shifting of shares from temporary ISIN to live ISIN T+2
21. Transfer of funds from Rights escrow account to Issuer’s monitoring account T+2
22. Commencement of trading of shares issued pursuant to rights issue T+3
23. Submission of media compliance report with SEI T+3

items.html:
TABLE B: Indicative timeline of broad activities involved in Rights Issue from the date of closure of Rights Issue till the date of listing and trading
Sr. No. Broad activities performed during Rights Issue Process Timelines (Working Days)
1. Issue closure T (T being the date of closure of issue)
2. Suspension of RE ISIN (immediately on issue closure) T
3. RTA obtains bid file from Stock Exchanges (SEs) T
4. Reverting for correction files to SCSBs for ASBA bids T
5. Receiving rectified/final bid data from SEs T
6. Receipt of final certificate from the SCSBs T
7. Co-ordination with SCSBs for pending final certificates for ASBA application T
8. Complete reconciliation of valid ASBA, REs holding and technical rejection. T
9. Basis of allotment to be carried out by RTAs in coordination with Designated Stock Exchange (DSE) T+1
10. Stock Exchange to approve the basis of allotment T+1
11. Transfer of funds from ASBA accounts to allotment account for allottees and refund account and unblocking of accounts for non-allottees T+1
12. Listing application to be made to SEs T+1
13. Instructions to dispatch of allotment and refund/unblocking intimations T+2
14. Receiving in-principle listing approval for corporate action T+2
15. Submit application with depositories for credit to respective demat shareholder account T+2
16. Receipt of credit confirmation from NSDL/CDSL T+2
17. Filing Documents with SEs for trading approval T+2
18. Publication of basis of allotment advertisement in newspaper and submission of same with SEs T+2
19. Receipt of trading approval from SEs T+2
20. Shifting of shares from temporary ISIN to live ISIN T+2
21. Transfer of funds from Rights escrow account to Issuer’s monitoring account T+2
22. Commencement of trading of shares issued pursuant to rights issue T+3
23. Submission of media compliance report with SEI T+3

items.value: <u>Procedures on the Rights Issue process</u>
items.md: # <u>Procedures on the Rights Issue process</u>

items.value: A. Application Form
items.md: A. Application Form

items.value: a) The issuer shall dispatch a common application form to its shareholders as on the record date. Along with application form, the issuer shall also send the details of the rights entitlements of the shareholder separately.
items.md: a) The issuer shall dispatch a common application form to its shareholders as on the record date. Along with application form, the issuer shall also send the details of the rights entitlements of the shareholder separately.

items.value: b) This application form can be used both by shareholder or renouncee.
items.md: b) This application form can be used both by shareholder or renouncee.

items.value: c) Registrar to the issue shall also upload the application forms on its website.
items.md: c) Registrar to the issue shall also upload the application forms on its website.

items.value: d) Applicants can use application form available on the website of registrar to the issue or printed forms sourced from the issuer <mark>[***]<sup>75</sup></mark> or registrars to the issue.
items.md: d) Applicants can use application form available on the website of registrar to the issue or printed forms sourced from the issuer <mark>\[\*\*\*]<sup>75</sup></mark> or registrars to the issue.

items.value: e) In terms of <u>
items.md: e) In terms of <u>
[Regulation 78](https://www.sebi.gov.in/web/?file=https://www.sebi.gov.in/sebi_data/attachdocs/nov-2025/1762938017891.pdf#page=74&zoom=page-width,-15,246)

items.value: </u> of the ICDR Regulations 2018, investor also has option to make an application in writing on a plain paper.
items.md: </u> of the ICDR Regulations 2018, investor also has option to make an application in writing on a plain paper.

items.value: B. Credit of Rights Entitlements (“REs”) in dematerialized form
items.md: B. Credit of Rights Entitlements (“REs”) in dematerialized form

items.value: a) The depositories shall put necessary procedures in place for issue and credit of REs in demat mode.
items.md: a) The depositories shall put necessary procedures in place for issue and credit of REs in demat mode.

items.value: b) The issuer making a rights issue of specified securities shall ensure that it has made necessary arrangements with depositories to issue and credit the REs in demat mode in the demat accounts of shareholders holding shares as on the record date.
items.md: b) The issuer making a rights issue of specified securities shall ensure that it has made necessary arrangements with depositories to issue and credit the REs in demat mode in the demat accounts of shareholders holding shares as on the record date.

items.value: c) A separate ISIN shall be obtained by the issuer for credit of REs.
items.md: c) A separate ISIN shall be obtained by the issuer for credit of REs.

items.value: d) Issuer shall specify the ISIN for REs while announcing the record date.
items.md: d) Issuer shall specify the ISIN for REs while announcing the record date.

items.value: e) Based on the rights entitlement ratio, the issuer shall credit REs in dematerialized mode through corporate action to shareholders holding shares as on record date. The ISIN of REs shall be kept frozen (for debit) in the depository system till the date of opening of the issue.
items.md: e) Based on the rights entitlement ratio, the issuer shall credit REs in dematerialized mode through corporate action to shareholders holding shares as on record date. The ISIN of REs shall be kept frozen (for debit) in the depository system till the date of opening of the issue.

items.value: f) Physical shareholders shall be required to provide their demat account details to Issuer / Registrar to the Issue for credit of REs not later than two working days prior to issue closing date, such that credit of REs in their demat account takes place at least one day before issue closing date.
items.md: f) Physical shareholders shall be required to provide their demat account details to Issuer / Registrar to the Issue for credit of REs not later than two working days prior to issue closing date, such that credit of REs in their demat account takes place at least one day before issue closing date.

items.value: g) In case of fractional entitlements of REs, the fractional part shall be ignored by rounding down the entitlement.
items.md: g) In case of fractional entitlements of REs, the fractional part shall be ignored by rounding down the entitlement.

items.value: h) The issuer shall submit details of total REs credited to the stock exchanges immediately after completing the corporate action for the same and shall obtain requisite trading approval from the stock exchanges.
items.md: h) The issuer shall submit details of total REs credited to the stock exchanges immediately after completing the corporate action for the same and shall obtain requisite trading approval from the stock exchanges.

items.value: i) The details with respect to shareholder entitlement shall be made available on the website of the Registrar to the issue and the investors shall be able to check their respective entitlements on the website of the Registrar by keying their details, after adequate security controls to ensure that investors’ information is made available only to the particular investor. Issuer shall also carry these links on their website.
items.md: i) The details with respect to shareholder entitlement shall be made available on the website of the Registrar to the issue and the investors shall be able to check their respective entitlements on the website of the Registrar by keying their details, after adequate security controls to ensure that investors’ information is made available only to the particular investor. Issuer shall also carry these links on their website.

items.value: j) If the demat account of a shareholder is frozen or demat account details are not available, including shares held in unclaimed suspense account or in the account of IEPF Authority, then REs shall be credited in a suspense escrow demat account of the Company and an intimation should be sent to such shareholder by the issuer /Registrar to the issue.
items.md: j) If the demat account of a shareholder is frozen or demat account details are not available, including shares held in unclaimed suspense account or in the account of IEPF Authority, then REs shall be credited in a suspense escrow demat account of the Company and an intimation should be sent to such shareholder by the issuer /Registrar to the issue.

items.value: k) The issuer shall intimate issue closing date to the depositories at least one day before the issue closing date, and the depositories shall suspend the ISIN of REs for transfers, from issue closing date.
items.md: k) The issuer shall intimate issue closing date to the depositories at least one day before the issue closing date, and the depositories shall suspend the ISIN of REs for transfers, from issue closing date.

items.value: l) REs which are neither renounced nor subscribed by the shareholders, shall be lapsed after closure of the Rights Issue.
items.md: l) REs which are neither renounced nor subscribed by the shareholders, shall be lapsed after closure of the Rights Issue.

items.value: m) Issuer Company shall ensure that REs which are lapsed are extinguished from the depository system once securities are allotted pursuant to Rights Issue. Once allotment is done, the ISIN for REs shall be permanently deactivated in the depository system by the depositories.
items.md: m) Issuer Company shall ensure that REs which are lapsed are extinguished from the depository system once securities are allotted pursuant to Rights Issue. Once allotment is done, the ISIN for REs shall be permanently deactivated in the depository system by the depositories.

items.value: C. Renunciation process and trading of REs on stock exchange platform:
items.md: **C. Renunciation process and trading of REs on stock exchange platform:**

items.value: a) The stock exchanges shall put necessary procedures in place for trading of REs on stock exchange platform.
items.md: a) The stock exchanges shall put necessary procedures in place for trading of REs on stock exchange platform.

items.value: b) REs credited to demat account can be renounced either by sale of REs using stock exchanges platform or off-market transfer and such trades will be settled by transferring dematerialized REs through depository mechanism in the same manner as done for all other types of securities.
items.md: b) REs credited to demat account can be renounced either by sale of REs using stock exchanges platform or off-market transfer and such trades will be settled by transferring dematerialized REs through depository mechanism in the same manner as done for all other types of securities.

items.value: c) For sale of REs through stock exchange, investors can place order for sale of REs only to the extent of REs available in the demat account of the investor. Trading in REs on the secondary market platform of Stock exchanges will happen electronically on T+1 rolling settlement basis where T being the date of trading. The transactions will be settled on trade-for-trade basis.
items.md: c) For sale of REs through stock exchange, investors can place order for sale of REs only to the extent of REs available in the demat account of the investor. Trading in REs on the secondary market platform of Stock exchanges will happen electronically on T+1 rolling settlement basis where T being the date of trading. The transactions will be settled on trade-for-trade basis.

items.md: d) Issuer shall inform the dates of issue opening and closing to the stock exchanges and the depositories at the time of filing the letter of offer with the stock exchanges.

items.value: e) Trading in REs shall commence on the date of opening of the issue and shall be closed at least three working days prior to the closure of rights issue.

items.md: D. Submission of Application form in Rights Issue

items.value: a) All investors (including renouncee) shall submit application forms using ASBA facility through the Self Certified Syndicate Banks (SCSB) network during the issue period.
items.md: a) All investors (including renouncee) shall submit application forms using ASBA facility through the Self Certified Syndicate Banks (SCSB) network during the issue period.

items.value: b) Investor shall submit only one application form for REs available in a particular demat account.
items.md: b) Investor shall submit only one application form for REs available in a particular demat account.

items.value: E. Allotment process in the rights issue
items.md: E. Allotment process in the rights issue

items.value: a) [Correction of bid data as collated by the SCSB after issue closing shall be completed on the issue closure date itself].<sup>76</sup>
items.md: a) \[Correction of bid data as collated by the SCSB after issue closing shall be completed on the issue closure date itself].<sup>76</sup>

items.value: b) Registrar shall obtain demographic details of all applicants from depositories.
items.md: b) Registrar shall obtain demographic details of all applicants from depositories.

items.value: c) Registrar shall obtain details of holders of REs as on issue closing date, from the depositories.
items.md: c) Registrar shall obtain details of holders of REs as on issue closing date, from the depositories.

items.value: d) After reconciliation of valid ASBA applications, funds blocked and REs demat holding list, the registrar shall finalise allocation of securities offered through rights offering.
items.md: d) After reconciliation of valid ASBA applications, funds blocked and REs demat holding list, the registrar shall finalise allocation of securities offered through rights offering.

items.value: e) Registrar shall credit the shares to the respective demat accounts of the applicants based on basis of allotment approved by the designated stock exchange and shall issue instructions to unblock bank accounts wherever necessary.
items.md: e) Registrar shall credit the shares to the respective demat accounts of the applicants based on basis of allotment approved by the designated stock exchange and shall issue instructions to unblock bank accounts wherever necessary.

items.value: Investment in equity & equity-related securities involve a degree of risk and investors should not invest any funds in this Issue unless they can afford to take the risk of losing their investment. Investors are advised to read the risk factors carefully before taking an investment decision in this Issue. For taking an investment decision, investors must rely on their own examination of the Issuer and this Issue, including the risks involved. The Equity Shares have not been recommended or approved by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (“SEBI”), nor does, SEBI guarantee the accuracy or adequacy of the contents of the RHP. Specified attention of the investors is invited to the section titled “Risk Factors” at page XXX of the RHP and on page XX of this Abridged Prospectus.

items.value: GENERAL RISK
items.md: ## GENERAL RISK

items.value: Investments in equity and equity-related securities involve a degree of risk and investors should not invest any funds in the Offer unless they can afford to take the risk of losing their entire investment. Investors are advised to read the risk factors carefully before taking an investment decision in the Offer. For taking an investment decision, investors must rely on their own examination of our Company and the Offer, including the risks involved. The Equity Shares in the Offer have not been recommended or approved by the Securities and Exchange Board of India ("SEBI"), nor does SEBI guarantee the accuracy or adequacy of the contents of this Draft Red Herring Prospectus. Specific attention of the investors is invited to "Risk Factors" on page [•].

items.value: Annexure IIIA
items.md: Annexure IIIA

items.value: <u>Guidelines for returning of draft offer document and its resubmission</u>
items.md: # <u>Guidelines for returning of draft offer document and its resubmission</u>

items.value: 1. <u>Return of Draft Offer Document:</u>
items.md: ## 1. <u>Return of Draft Offer Document:</u>

items.value: The draft offer document []<sup>77</sup>filed with the Board for public issue []<sup>78</sup>(hereinafter “draft offer document”) of securities shall be scrutinized based on the broad guidelines specified as under and accordingly, the draft offer document shall be returned to the Issuer and the Lead Manager(s) for resubmission in accordance with the following guidelines -
items.md: The draft offer document \[***]<sup>77</sup>filed with the Board for public issue \[***]<sup>78</sup>(hereinafter “draft offer document”) of securities shall be scrutinized based on the broad guidelines specified as under and accordingly, the draft offer document shall be returned to the Issuer and the Lead Manager(s) for resubmission in accordance with the following guidelines -

items.value: 1.1. Disclosures made in the draft offer document do not satisfy one or more of the following requirements:
items.md: 1.1. Disclosures made in the draft offer document do not satisfy one or more of the following requirements:

items.value: i. Draft offer document is drafted in simple language with visual representation of data, so as to ensure ease of understanding of its contents and which substantially complies with the following principles:
items.md: i. Draft offer document is drafted in simple language with visual representation of data, so as to ensure ease of understanding of its contents and which substantially complies with the following principles:

items.value: a) Use of short sentences;
items.md: a) Use of short sentences;

items.value: b) Use of definite, unambiguous and conventional words;
items.md: b) Use of definite, unambiguous and conventional words;

items.value: c) Use of active voice;
items.md: c) Use of active voice;

items.value: d) Use of tabular presentation or bullet lists, where required; and
items.md: d) Use of tabular presentation or bullet lists, where required; and

items.value: e) Avoidance of multiple negatives.
items.md: e) Avoidance of multiple negatives.

items.value: ii. The information in the draft offer document is presented in a clear, concise, and intelligible manner, adhering to the following standards:
items.md: ii. The information in the draft offer document is presented in a clear, concise, and intelligible manner, adhering to the following standards:

items.value: a) Clear and concise sections, paragraphs and sentences;
items.md: a) Clear and concise sections, paragraphs and sentences;

items.value: b) Descriptive headings and subheadings wherever necessary;
items.md: b) Descriptive headings and subheadings wherever necessary;

items.value: c) Avoidance of legal and technical terminology; and
items.md: c) Avoidance of legal and technical terminology; and

items.value: d) Clarification of technical and complex terms, if any, used to explain the business of the issuer/other matters in simple terms.
items.md: d) Clarification of technical and complex terms, if any, used to explain the business of the issuer/other matters in simple terms.

items.value: iii. The draft offer document avoids:
items.md: iii. The draft offer document avoids:

items.value: a) Complex presentations that may make the substance of the disclosures incomprehensible;
items.md: a) Complex presentations that may make the substance of the disclosures incomprehensible;

items.value: b) Vague, ambiguous and imprecise explanations which may lead to more than one interpretation;
items.md: b) Vague, ambiguous and imprecise explanations which may lead to more than one interpretation;

items.value: *77 Omitted vide SEBI circular no, <u>
items.md: \*77 Omitted vide SEBI circular no, <u>
[SEBI/HO/CFD/CFD-PoD-1/P/CIR/2025/31](https://www.sebi.gov.in/legal/circulars/mar-2025/faster-rights-issue-with-a-flexibility-of-allotment-to-specific-investor-s-_92622.html)

items.value: </u> date March 11, 2025. Before omission it read as “draft letter of offer”
items.md: </u> date March 11, 2025. Before omission it read as “*draft letter of offer*”

items.value: *78 Omitted vide SEBI circular no, <u>
items.md: \*78 Omitted vide SEBI circular no, <u>
[SEBI/HO/CFD/CFD-PoD-1/P/CIR/2025/31](https://www.sebi.gov.in/legal/circulars/mar-2025/faster-rights-issue-with-a-flexibility-of-allotment-to-specific-investor-s-_92622.html)

items.value: c) Complex information quoted or copied from legal documents, unless accompanied with clear and concise explanation of the provision(s) therein;
items.md: c) Complex information quoted or copied from legal documents, unless accompanied with clear and concise explanation of the provision(s) therein;

items.value: d) Repetition of disclosures in different sections of the document which may increase the size of the document but does not improve the quality or efficacy of information, unless the context requires otherwise; and
items.md: d) Repetition of disclosures in different sections of the document which may increase the size of the document but does not improve the quality or efficacy of information, unless the context requires otherwise; and

items.value: e) Inconsistency in the numbers/ data/ facts provided in different sections of the offer document or between the draft offer document and subsequent submission(s) made in response to clarifications sought.
items.md: e) Inconsistency in the numbers/ data/ facts provided in different sections of the offer document or between the draft offer document and subsequent submission(s) made in response to clarifications sought.

items.value: iv. The risk factors are appropriately worded in simple, clear and unambiguous language to bring out clearly the risk to the investor, without undermining the same.
items.md: iv. The risk factors are appropriately worded in simple, clear and unambiguous language to bring out clearly the risk to the investor, without undermining the same.

items.value: 1.2. The draft offer document requires:
items.md: 1.2. The draft offer document requires:

items.value: i. Substantial revision or addenda on key disclosures, in accordance with the clarifications / explanations sought on the draft offer document; or
items.md: i. Substantial revision or addenda on key disclosures, in accordance with the clarifications / explanations sought on the draft offer document; or

items.value: ii. Corrective measures on account of regulatory interpretation.
items.md: ii. Corrective measures on account of regulatory interpretation.

items.value: 1.3. The information required to be included in the draft offer document is not clearly understandable without the necessity of referring to the general rules and regulations.
items.md: 1.3. The information required to be included in the draft offer document is not clearly understandable without the necessity of referring to the general rules and regulations.

items.value: Illustration: If an issue is filed under regulation 6(2) of the regulations, the draft offer document shall clearly specify the exact requirement under regulation 6(1) which has not been met by the issuer, instead of merely stating that the document is filed under regulation 6(2) of the Regulations.
items.md: *Illustration: If an issue is filed under regulation 6(2) of the regulations, the draft offer document shall clearly specify the exact requirement under regulation 6(1) which has not been met by the issuer, instead of merely stating that the document is filed under regulation 6(2) of the Regulations.*

items.value: 1.4. Where any other regulatory authority/enforcement agency has expressed material concern with regard to issue / draft offer document filed by the issuer.
items.md: 1.4. Where any other regulatory authority/enforcement agency has expressed material concern with regard to issue / draft offer document filed by the issuer.

items.value: On return of the draft offer document, the issuer shall ensure to seek/undertake remedial measures with the relevant regulator prior to resubmission of the document.
items.md: *On return of the draft offer document, the issuer shall ensure to seek/undertake remedial measures with the relevant regulator prior to resubmission of the document.*

items.value: 1.5. Where there are any pending litigation matters in any court or tribunal having an impact on the issue with regard to eligibility criteria provided under ICDR Regulations for the issue / draft offer document filed by the issuer.
items.md: 1.5. Where there are any pending litigation matters in any court or tribunal having an impact on the issue with regard to eligibility criteria provided under ICDR Regulations for the issue / draft offer document filed by the issuer.

items.value: For purpose of eligibility criteria as mentioned in above para, Regulation 5, Regulation 61, Regulation 102, Regulation 213 and Regulation 228 of ICDR Regulation may be referred.
items.md: *For purpose of eligibility criteria as mentioned in above para, Regulation 5, Regulation 61, Regulation 102, Regulation 213 and Regulation 228 of ICDR Regulation may be referred.*

items.value: <u>Resubmission of Draft Offer Document:</u>
items.md: <u>**Resubmission of Draft Offer Document:**</u>
2. <u>**Resubmission of Draft Offer Document:**</u>

items.value: 2.1. While there shall be no requirement for payment of any fees on account of resubmission of draft offer document, the requirement for paying applicable fees for the changes, if any, in terms of changes specified in Schedule XVI of the ICDR Regulations for the updated
items.md: 2.1. While there shall be no requirement for payment of any fees on account of resubmission of draft offer document, the requirement for paying applicable fees for the changes, if any, in terms of changes specified in Schedule XVI of the ICDR Regulations for the updated

items.value: offer document shall continue to apply as is applicable to issuer for updation in offer document.
items.md: offer document shall continue to apply as is applicable to issuer for updation in offer document.

items.value: 2.2. There shall be no refund of the filing fees on account of non-submission of draft offer document by the issuer after return.
items.md: 2.2. There shall be no refund of the filing fees on account of non-submission of draft offer document by the issuer after return.

items.value: 2.3. The issuer, within two days of resubmission of draft offer document with the Board, shall make a public announcement in the mode and manner as specified under ICDR Regulations, as applicable, and the issuer shall also include a disclosure that it is a resubmitted document.
items.md: 2.3. The issuer, within two days of resubmission of draft offer document with the Board, shall make a public announcement in the mode and manner as specified under ICDR Regulations, as applicable, and the issuer shall also include a disclosure that it is a resubmitted document.

items.value: 2.4. Issuer shall make written intimation to its sectoral regulator, if any, informing about the return and resubmission of the draft offer document, as applicable.
items.md: 2.4. Issuer shall make written intimation to its sectoral regulator, if any, informing about the return and resubmission of the draft offer document, as applicable.

items.value: The Issuer and the Lead Manager(s) shall ensure that the draft offer document is resubmitted only after addressing insufficiency for which the draft offer document was returned and such draft offer document is in compliance with provisions of ICDR Regulations and other applicable laws.
items.md: The Issuer and the Lead Manager(s) shall ensure that the draft offer document is resubmitted only after addressing insufficiency for which the draft offer document was returned and such draft offer document is in compliance with provisions of ICDR Regulations and other applicable laws.

items.value: For recognition as an Innovators Growth Platform Investor for the purpose of Innovators Growth Platform, the investor having a demat account with a Depository shall submit the following documents with the Stock Exchanges/Depositories or Brokers/Depository Participants:
items.md: For recognition as an Innovators Growth Platform Investor for the purpose of Innovators Growth Platform, the investor having a demat account with a Depository shall submit the following documents with the Stock Exchanges/Depositories or Brokers/Depository Participants:

items.value: I. In case of Individual / HUF
items.md: ## I. In case of Individual / HUF

items.value: i) Copy of PAN Card.
items.md: i) Copy of PAN Card.

items.value: ii) Copy of Aadhaar Card or Copy of Valid Passport.
items.md: ii) Copy of Aadhaar Card or Copy of Valid Passport.

items.value: iii) Income tax return of last 3 financial years
items.md: iii) Income tax return of last 3 financial years

items.value: iv) Certificate from practicing chartered accountant stating total gross income (annually) and liquid net worth as on date of application. Working of Liquid Net worth shall be given as an Annexure to the certificate. The same shall be calculated as follows:
items.md: iv) Certificate from practicing chartered accountant stating total gross income (annually) and liquid net worth as on date of application. Working of Liquid Net worth shall be given as an Annexure to the certificate. The same shall be calculated as follows:

items.value:
Capital + Free Reserve
Less: non allowable assets
items.md:
Capital + Free Reserve
Less: non allowable assets

items.value: (a) Fixed assets
items.md: (a) Fixed assets

items.value: (b) Pledged Securities
items.md: (b) Pledged Securities

items.value: (c) Non-allowable securities (unlisted securities)
items.md: (c) Non-allowable securities (unlisted securities)

items.value: (d) Doubtful debts and advances*
items.md: (d) Doubtful debts and advances\*

items.value: (e) Prepaid expenses, losses
items.md: (e) Prepaid expenses, losses

items.value: (f) Intangible assets
items.md: (f) Intangible assets

items.value: (g) 30% value of marketable securities
items.md: (g) 30% value of marketable securities

items.value: * Explanation – Includes debts/advances overdue for more than three months or given to associates and to related parties.
items.md: \* Explanation – Includes debts/advances overdue for more than three months or given to associates and to related parties.

items.value: v) Where the individual has been debarred or disciplinary action has been taken against investor by SEBI, RBI or any other regulatory body, then the debarment period or disciplinary action should be over. In case of a Non Resident Indian, he or she shall confirm that he or she has not been restricted from accessing securities market by the country of jurisdiction where he or she resides.
items.md: v) Where the individual has been debarred or disciplinary action has been taken against investor by SEBI, RBI or any other regulatory body, then the debarment period or disciplinary action should be over.
